regulationName: General Pretreatment Regulations — Prohibited Discharge Standards (40 CFR 403.5)
chineseTitle: 联邦预处理条例——禁止排放标准（油脂阻塞）
englishTitle: Federal Pretreatment Prohibited Discharges — FOG / Obstruction of POTW Flow
applicabilityThreshold: All non-domestic users discharging to a POTW (any food-service establishment). 所有向市政污水系统排放的非生活污水用户（含餐饮店）。
keyRequirements: No user may discharge to a public sewer (POTW) any pollutant causing pass-through or interference, including solid or viscous pollutants such as fats, oils and grease in amounts that obstruct flow.
chineseSummary: 根据《清洁水法》制定的联邦预处理条例，40 CFR 403.5(a) 禁止任何用户向城市污水处理系统（POTW）排放造成穿透或干扰的污染物；403.5(b)(3) 明确禁止可能堵塞管道造成干扰的固态或粘稠污染物（包括油脂 FOG）。该全国性禁令适用于所有向 POTW 排放的非生活污水源，无论当地是否已建立预处理计划，是各地市政油脂阻隔器（grease trap/interceptor）要求的联邦法律基础。
englishSummary: Promulgated under the Clean Water Act, the federal general pretreatment rule at 40 CFR 403.5(a) bars any user from discharging pollutants that cause pass-through or interference at a publicly owned treatment works (POTW); 403.5(b)(3) specifically prohibits solid or viscous pollutants — including fats, oils and grease — in amounts that obstruct flow. These national prohibitions apply to every non-domestic discharger regardless of whether the local POTW has its own pretreatment program, and form the federal legal basis underlying local grease-interceptor mandates.
businessImpact: Every café kitchen discharging dishwashing and coffee-equipment wastewater is a non-domestic user that must keep FOG out of the sewer, the federal anchor for local grease-trap rules in every expansion market.
recommendedAction: Install and service grease interceptors per local POTW limits in every new location; retain pump-out/manifest records. 在每个新门店安装并定期清掏隔油池，保留清运记录。

regulationName: Energy Policy Act of 1992 — Water Efficiency Standards for Plumbing Fixtures
chineseTitle: 1992年能源政策法——管道器具节水标准
englishTitle: EPAct 1992 Federal Water Efficiency Standards for Plumbing Fixtures
applicabilityThreshold: All newly installed plumbing fixtures in commercial buildings nationwide. 全美所有新安装的商用管道器具。
keyRequirements: Sets enforceable national maximum water-use standards for plumbing fixtures — toilets 1.6 gpf, urinals 1.0 gpf, faucets 2.2 gpm and showerheads 2.5 gpm — that all newly manufactured/installed fixtures must meet.
chineseSummary: 1992年《能源政策法》（EPAct，1992年10月24日签署）首次设立全国强制性管道器具用水效率标准，对马桶（1.6 加仑/冲）、小便器、水龙头（2.2 加仑/分）和淋浴喷头（2.5 加仑/分）规定最大用水量，自1994年起对新制造和安装的器具生效。各地公共卫生间龙头标准后被国家管道规范进一步收紧至 0.5 加仑/分。这是美国节水法规的基础性联邦法律。
englishSummary: The Energy Policy Act of 1992 (signed Oct 24, 1992) established the first enforceable national water-efficiency standards for plumbing fixtures — 1.6 gpf toilets, 1.0 gpf urinals, 2.2 gpm faucets and 2.5 gpm showerheads — applicable to newly manufactured and installed fixtures from 1994. Public-lavatory faucet limits were later tightened to 0.5 gpm by national plumbing codes. It is the foundational federal water-conservation statute.
businessImpact: Café fit-outs must specify compliant low-flow faucets and toilets; non-compliant fixtures fail plumbing inspection and block the certificate of occupancy.
recommendedAction: Spec EPAct/WaterSense-compliant fixtures in build-out drawings; verify with the plumbing inspector at each new store. 在装修图纸中指定合规节水器具并经管道验收。

regulationName: Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007 — General Service Lamp Efficiency Standards
chineseTitle: 2007年能源独立与安全法——通用照明灯具能效标准
englishTitle: EISA 2007 General Service Lamp Efficiency Standards (45 lumens/watt)
applicabilityThreshold: Manufacturers/retailers of general service lamps nationwide; affects all lamp buyers. 全美通用照明灯具制造商/零售商，影响所有采购方。
keyRequirements: Prohibits manufacture and sale of general service lamps that do not meet the minimum 45 lumens-per-watt efficiency standard, effectively phasing out most common incandescent bulbs as of August 1, 2023.
chineseSummary: 2007年《能源独立与安全法》（EISA，2007年12月19日签署）为通用照明灯泡设定最低能效标准。DOE 自2023年8月1日起全面执行 45 流明/瓦的标准，禁止制造和销售大多数传统白炽灯泡，实际推动门店转向 LED 照明。DOE 另有2028年生效的更高标准的最终规则。这是一项约束制造商/经销商、间接影响门店采购的能效法规。
englishSummary: The Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007 (signed Dec 19, 2007) set minimum efficiency standards for general service lamps. DOE began full enforcement of the 45 lumens-per-watt standard on August 1, 2023, banning manufacture and sale of most common incandescent bulbs and effectively pushing stores toward LED lighting; a further DOE final rule raises standards for compliance in 2028. It binds manufacturers/retailers and indirectly shapes store lamp purchasing.
businessImpact: Café lighting replacements must use LED/compliant lamps — incandescent stockpiling is no longer a sourcing option and bulbs purchased post-2023 must meet the standard.
recommendedAction: Standardize on LED fixtures chain-wide; treat any spent fluorescent tubes as universal waste (see RCRA lamp rule). 全店统一改用 LED；废旧荧光灯管按通用废物处理。

regulationName: RCRA Universal Waste Rule — Hazardous Waste Lamps (40 CFR Part 273)
chineseTitle: RCRA通用废物规则——含汞灯管（40 CFR 273）
englishTitle: RCRA Universal Waste Rule — Spent Fluorescent / Mercury Lamps
applicabilityThreshold: Any generator of spent mercury lamps; small-quantity handler if under 5,000 kg accumulated. 任何产生废汞灯管者；积存少于5,000公斤为小量处理者。
keyRequirements: Spent mercury-containing lamps must be managed as universal waste — kept intact, labeled, stored on-site no longer than one year, and sent to a recycler/permitted facility rather than the trash.
chineseSummary: EPA 通用废物规则（40 CFR Part 273，1995年制定，1999年将含汞灯管纳入，64 FR 36466）允许商家以简化方式管理废旧荧光灯等含汞灯管。小量处理者（积存少于5,000公斤）须保持灯管完整、贴标、现场存放不超过一年，并送至回收商而非作为普通垃圾丢弃。咖啡店更换荧光灯管即触发该规则。
englishSummary: EPA's Universal Waste Rule (40 CFR Part 273, established 1995; lamps added in 1999 via 64 FR 36466) lets businesses manage spent mercury-containing lamps under streamlined standards. Small-quantity handlers (under 5,000 kg accumulated) must keep lamps intact, labeled, stored on-site no longer than one year, and routed to a recycler rather than the trash. Replacing fluorescent tubes in a café triggers the rule.
businessImpact: Tossing burned-out fluorescent tubes in the dumpster is a federal hazardous-waste violation; cafés must arrange lamp recycling.
recommendedAction: Set up a labeled lamp-recycling box per store and contract a universal-waste hauler; favor LED to minimize generation. 每店设置贴标灯管回收箱并签约回收商；优先 LED 以减少产生。

regulationName: Oil Pollution Prevention — Spill Prevention, Control, and Countermeasure (SPCC) Rule (40 CFR Part 112)
chineseTitle: 石油污染防治——溢漏预防控制与应对规则（40 CFR 112）
englishTitle: EPA SPCC Rule — Oil/Animal-Fat & Grease Storage (40 CFR 112)
applicabilityThreshold: Aggregate aboveground oil/grease storage capacity over 1,320 gallons with discharge potential. 地上油脂储存总量超1,320加仑且有排放可能。
keyRequirements: Facilities with aggregate aboveground oil storage over 1,320 gallons (in containers 55 gallons or larger) and a reasonable potential to discharge to navigable waters must prepare and implement an SPCC plan; 'oil' includes animal fats, vegetable oils and greases.
chineseSummary: EPA 依《清洁水法》制定的 SPCC 规则（40 CFR Part 112）要求：地上石油类储存总量超过1,320加仑（单容器≥55加仑）且可能排入通航水体的设施，须编制并实施溢漏预防计划。该规则对‘油’的定义很广，包括动物油脂、植物油和油脂——因此大量回收用过食用油（UCO/黄油脂）罐的中央厨房或配送点可能被纳入。多数单体小型咖啡店通常低于阈值。
englishSummary: EPA's SPCC rule (40 CFR Part 112, under the Clean Water Act) requires facilities with aggregate aboveground oil storage exceeding 1,320 gallons (in containers of 55 gallons or more) and a reasonable potential to discharge to navigable waters to prepare and implement a spill-prevention plan. Critically, 'oil' is defined broadly to include animal fats, vegetable oils and greases — so a commissary or hub storing large used-cooking-oil (yellow-grease) totes can be captured. Most single small cafés fall below the threshold.
businessImpact: A roastery, commissary, or grease-storage hub aggregating used cooking oil can cross the 1,320-gallon line and owe an SPCC plan, even though individual storefronts usually do not.
recommendedAction: Tally aggregate oil/grease container capacity at any commissary or hub; if over 1,320 gal, have a PE-certified SPCC plan prepared. 核算配送中心油脂容器总容量，超1,320加仑须编制 SPCC 计划。

regulationName: NPDES 2021 Multi-Sector General Permit for Industrial Stormwater (MSGP)
chineseTitle: NPDES 2021多行业通用许可——工业雨水排放
englishTitle: EPA 2021 Multi-Sector General Permit — Industrial Stormwater
applicabilityThreshold: Facilities in 29 covered industrial sectors with stormwater discharges associated with industrial activity. 29类受覆盖工业行业且有工业雨水排放的设施。
keyRequirements: Facilities in covered industrial sectors that discharge stormwater associated with industrial activity must obtain permit coverage, develop a stormwater pollution prevention plan (SWPPP), and implement control measures and monitoring.
chineseSummary: EPA 依《清洁水法》第402条颁发的2021年多行业通用许可（MSGP，2021年3月1日生效，2026年2月28日到期后行政延续）覆盖29类工业活动设施的工业相关雨水排放，要求申请许可、编制雨水污染防治计划（SWPPP）并实施控制与监测措施。普通门店通常不属于受覆盖工业类别；但若咖啡连锁设有大型烘焙厂或配送/车辆维护场地暴露于雨水，则可能触发。
englishSummary: Issued under Clean Water Act Section 402, EPA's 2021 Multi-Sector General Permit (effective March 1, 2021; administratively continued after its Feb 28, 2026 expiration) covers stormwater associated with industrial activity at facilities in 29 industrial sectors, requiring permit coverage, a Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plan (SWPPP), and control measures plus monitoring. Ordinary storefronts are not a covered industrial category, but a large roasting plant or a fleet/maintenance yard with outdoor exposure could be pulled in.
businessImpact: A central roasting facility or distribution/vehicle yard with materials exposed to rain may need MSGP coverage and a SWPPP, a real obligation as the chain scales its supply chain.
recommendedAction: Assess whether any roasting/distribution site is an industrial sector with stormwater exposure; if so seek MSGP coverage and write a SWPPP. 评估烘焙/配送场地是否属受覆盖工业类别，必要时申请 MSGP 并编制 SWPPP。

regulationName: AIM Act Technology Transitions Rule — HFC Restrictions in Refrigeration (40 CFR Part 84)
chineseTitle: AIM法案技术转型规则——制冷设备HFC限制
englishTitle: AIM Act HFC Technology Transitions — Refrigeration (GWP 150)
applicabilityThreshold: Newly manufactured/imported refrigeration equipment by sector and effective date. 按行业与生效日期适用的新制造/进口制冷设备。
keyRequirements: New stand-alone retail food refrigeration equipment may not be manufactured or imported using refrigerants with a global warming potential (GWP) of 150 or higher, effective January 1, 2025, under the AIM Act.
chineseSummary: 依据2020年《美国创新与制造法》（AIM Act），EPA 技术转型规则（40 CFR Part 84）按 GWP 限制各行业 HFC 使用。自2025年1月1日起，新制造或进口的独立式零售食品制冷设备（如展示冰柜、独立冷藏柜）所用制冷剂 GWP 不得≥150，推动行业转向 R-290 等低 GWP 制冷剂；自助式制冰机等的限制于2026—2027年陆续生效。EPA 已于2025年10月提出对部分要求的重新审议（拟议放宽）。
englishSummary: Under the American Innovation and Manufacturing Act of 2020, EPA's Technology Transitions Rule (40 CFR Part 84) caps HFC use by sector based on global warming potential (GWP). Effective January 1, 2025, new stand-alone retail food refrigeration equipment (display coolers, self-contained reach-ins) may not be manufactured or imported using refrigerants with GWP of 150 or more, driving the market to low-GWP refrigerants like R-290; self-contained ice machine limits phase in across 2026-2027. EPA proposed a reconsideration (potential relief) in October 2025.
businessImpact: New display fridges, grab-and-go coolers and ice machines bought for store build-outs must use low-GWP refrigerants, affecting equipment specs and replacement sourcing chain-wide.
recommendedAction: Specify R-290/low-GWP refrigeration and ice machines in equipment standards; track the pending 2025 reconsideration. 设备标准中指定 R-290/低 GWP 制冷与制冰机；关注2025年拟议重新审议。

regulationName: FIFRA Pesticide Device Requirements — Establishment Registration & Labeling (40 CFR 167 / Part 152)
chineseTitle: FIFRA杀虫器械要求——场所登记与标识
englishTitle: FIFRA Pesticide Device — Producing-Establishment Registration & Labeling
applicabilityThreshold: Anyone producing or distributing a pesticidal device; users should verify compliance. 任何生产或分销杀虫器械者；使用者应核实合规。
keyRequirements: Pesticidal devices that work by physical or mechanical means (UV sanitizers, ozone generators, air purifiers, ultrasonic pest devices) need no product registration but must be produced in an EPA-registered establishment, bear the establishment number, and carry truthful, non-misbranded labeling.
chineseSummary: 依《联邦杀虫剂、杀菌剂和灭鼠剂法》（FIFRA），通过物理/机械方式起作用的杀虫器械（紫外线消毒器、臭氧发生器、空气净化器、超声驱虫器）无需产品登记，但须在 EPA 登记的生产场所制造、标注 EPA 场所编号，并使用真实、不得夸大功效（不得虚假声称杀灭细菌/病毒）的标签。EPA 近年对此类器械误标已发布多次合规警示并执法。咖啡店若使用此类设备需确保其合规标识。
englishSummary: Under the Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act (FIFRA), pesticidal devices that act by physical or mechanical means — UV sanitizers, ozone generators, air purifiers, ultrasonic pest repellers — do not require product registration but must be produced in an EPA-registered establishment, display the EPA establishment number, and carry truthful, non-misbranded labeling (no false kill claims). EPA has issued repeated compliance advisories and enforced against mislabeled devices.
businessImpact: Cafés deploying UV/ozone air-sanitizing or ultrasonic pest devices must verify each unit bears a valid EPA establishment number and makes no false germ-kill claims, or risk distributing a misbranded device.
recommendedAction: Before buying any UV/ozone/ultrasonic sanitizing device, confirm it shows an EPA establishment number and compliant labeling. 采购任何紫外线/臭氧/超声消毒器械前，确认标注 EPA 场所编号且标识合规。

regulationName: Designation of PFOA and PFOS as CERCLA Hazardous Substances
chineseTitle: 将PFOA和PFOS列为CERCLA有害物质
englishTitle: EPA PFAS CERCLA Hazardous Substance Designation (PFOA/PFOS)
applicabilityThreshold: Entities releasing/handling PFOA-PFOS at or above reportable quantity; broad supply-chain signal. 释放/处理达报告量的实体；对供应链有广泛信号意义。
keyRequirements: Designates PFOA and PFOS (two PFAS 'forever chemicals') as hazardous substances under CERCLA, triggering release-reporting to the National Response Center for releases meeting or exceeding one pound in 24 hours and creating Superfund cleanup liability.
chineseSummary: EPA 于2024年5月8日发布、7月8日生效的最终规则，首次依《超级基金法》（CERCLA）将两种 PFAS（PFOA 和 PFOS，‘永久化学品’）列为有害物质。凡24小时内释放达到或超过1磅须向国家应急中心报告，并产生 Superfund 清理责任。该规则主要针对制造商、机场、填埋场和水务，对咖啡店属间接/信息性影响（例如关注食品包装中的 PFAS 趋势）。EPA 已确认维持该认定。
englishSummary: EPA's final rule (issued May 8, 2024; effective July 8, 2024) for the first time designated two PFAS 'forever chemicals,' PFOA and PFOS, as hazardous substances under CERCLA (Superfund). Releases of one pound or more in 24 hours must be reported to the National Response Center, and the designation creates cleanup liability. The rule chiefly targets manufacturers, airports, landfills, and water utilities; for a café it is largely informational, signaling the broader regulatory push against PFAS in food packaging. EPA has confirmed it will retain the designation.
businessImpact: Not directly café-operative, but it accelerates the phase-out of PFAS-coated cups, wrappers and food packaging — a sourcing and supplier-vetting signal for the chain.
recommendedAction: Ask packaging suppliers for PFAS-free certifications to stay ahead of tightening federal and state PFAS rules. 要求包装供应商提供无 PFAS 认证，提前应对联邦及各州收紧。

regulationName: Lead and Copper Rule Improvements (LCRI) — National Primary Drinking Water Regulation
chineseTitle: 铅和铜规则改进（LCRI）——国家一级饮用水法规
englishTitle: EPA Lead and Copper Rule Improvements (LCRI)
applicabilityThreshold: Public water systems nationwide; café relevance is via building/service-line water quality. 全美公共供水系统；咖啡店通过建筑供水管水质相关。
keyRequirements: Requires water systems to replace lead service lines within 10 years and lowers the lead action level to 0.010 mg/L, with regulated entities required to comply beginning November 1, 2027.
chineseSummary: EPA 于2024年10月30日发布、2024年12月30日生效的《铅和铜规则改进》（LCRI）要求几乎所有供水系统在10年内更换全部铅水管，并将铅行动水平降至0.010 mg/L，强化腐蚀控制、取样及公众告知；受监管单位须自2027年11月1日起遵守。对咖啡店而言，门店龙头出水水质（尤其老旧建筑铅管）直接影响产品安全，是选址与租约尽职调查的关注点。
englishSummary: EPA's Lead and Copper Rule Improvements (published Oct 30, 2024; rule effective Dec 30, 2024) require nearly all water systems to replace lead service lines within ten years and lower the lead action level to 0.010 mg/L, while strengthening corrosion control, tap sampling, and public notification; regulated systems must comply beginning November 1, 2027. For a café, the quality of tap water at the point of use — especially lead piping in older buildings — directly affects product safety and is a site-selection and lease due-diligence concern.
businessImpact: Because every drink is made with tap water, a café leasing space in an older building should confirm there is no lead service line and watch its water utility's replacement timeline.
recommendedAction: During lease due diligence, ask whether the building has a lead service line; consider point-of-use filtration in older premises. 租约尽调时询问建筑是否有铅水管，老旧物业考虑加装末端过滤。

regulationName: National Strategy to Prevent Plastic Pollution
chineseTitle: 国家防治塑料污染战略
englishTitle: EPA National Strategy to Prevent Plastic Pollution
applicabilityThreshold: Voluntary, economy-wide; no binding threshold. 自愿性、全经济范围；无强制阈值。
keyRequirements: A voluntary national strategy identifying actions for governments, businesses and communities to eliminate the release of plastic waste into the environment by 2040; it sets objectives rather than binding mandates.
chineseSummary: EPA 于2023年4月发布草案、2024年11月21日发布最终版的《国家防治塑料污染战略》，是‘构建循环经济’系列的一部分。该战略为政府、企业、非营利组织和社区提出自愿性行动方向，目标是到2040年消除塑料废物向环境的释放，本身不具强制约束力，但预示联邦及各州未来对一次性塑料制品（餐具、杯子、吸管、包装）的收紧。对咖啡连锁是供应链和品牌可持续性的前瞻性信号。
englishSummary: EPA's National Strategy to Prevent Plastic Pollution (draft April 2023; final released Nov 21, 2024), part of the agency's 'Building a Circular Economy for All' series, sets out voluntary actions for governments, businesses, nonprofits and communities aimed at eliminating the release of plastic waste into the environment by 2040. It establishes objectives rather than binding mandates but signals the direction of future federal and state restrictions on single-use plastics — cutlery, cups, straws and packaging. For a café chain it is a forward-looking supply-chain and brand-sustainability signal.
businessImpact: Voluntary today, but it telegraphs the federal trajectory toward single-use plastic reduction, informing the chain's foodware sourcing and packaging strategy ahead of binding state/local bans.
recommendedAction: Use the strategy to guide a proactive shift to recyclable/compostable foodware before mandatory bans land in new markets. 据此前瞻性转向可回收/可堆肥餐具，抢先于强制禁令落地。

regulationName: Paper Carryout Bag Reduction Fee (Bag Waste Reduction Act)
chineseTitle: 纸质购物袋减量费（减袋法）
englishTitle: Paper Carryout Bag Reduction Fee
agency: New York State Department of Taxation and Finance (with NYSDEC); locally adopted by New York City
applicabilityThreshold: Any business required to collect NYS sales tax in a locality that adopted the fee (NYC). 在已采纳该费用地区（如纽约市）需征收销售税的所有商家。
keyRequirements: Sales-tax vendors (including food establishments) in localities that adopted the fee, such as NYC, must charge customers a 5-cent fee per paper carryout bag and remit it, with SNAP/WIC customers exempt.
chineseSummary: 依据纽约州《减袋法》（ECL第27条第28章），自2020年3月1日起全州禁止一次性塑料购物袋，并允许地方对纸袋征收每个5美分的减量费。纽约市已采纳该5美分纸袋费，所有需征收销售税的商家（含咖啡店等食品场所）在提供纸质外带袋时必须收取并上缴该费用，SNAP/WIC顾客可豁免。
englishSummary: Under New York's Bag Waste Reduction Act (ECL Art. 27, Title 28), single-use plastic carryout bags are banned statewide since March 1, 2020, and localities may impose a 5-cent paper-bag fee. NYC adopted the 5-cent paper-bag fee, so any sales-tax vendor, including food establishments, must charge and remit the fee on paper carryout bags, with SNAP/WIC customers exempt.
businessImpact: A cafe handing customers paper carryout bags must collect, track, and remit the 5-cent fee per bag or risk tax/penalty exposure, and cannot provide single-use plastic carryout bags.
recommendedAction: Configure POS to add the 5-cent paper-bag fee, exempt SNAP/WIC, and remit via sales-tax filings; stock only reusable or paper bags. 在POS系统中设置5美分纸袋费并按规上缴。

regulationName: 6 NYCRR Part 494 - Hydrofluorocarbon Standards and Reporting
chineseTitle: 氢氟碳化物（HFC）标准与报告规则（第494部分）
englishTitle: Hydrofluorocarbon (HFC) Standards and Reporting
agency: New York State Department of Environmental Conservation (NYSDEC)
applicabilityThreshold: Suppliers/installers of covered new refrigeration equipment; Refrigerant Management Program applies to equipment with 50 lbs or more refrigerant charge. 制冷剂管理计划适用于充注量达50磅及以上的设备。
keyRequirements: Prohibits newly manufactured or installed refrigeration equipment using high-GWP HFCs (e.g., retail food stand-alone refrigeration units restricted from Jan 9, 2025; refrigerated food dispensing equipment from Jan 1, 2027), and imposes a Refrigerant Management Program on owners of equipment containing 50 pounds or more of refrigerant.
chineseSummary: 纽约州依据《气候领导与社区保护法》于2025年1月修订了第494部分，分阶段禁止在新设备中使用高全球变暖潜值（GWP）的氢氟碳化物。规则直接涵盖零售食品独立式制冷设备（2025年1月9日起）和冷藏食品分配设备（2027年1月1日起）；持有充注量达50磅及以上制冷剂设备的业主还须遵守制冷剂管理计划的泄漏检测与记录要求。
englishSummary: NYSDEC's amended Part 494 (adopted January 2025 under the Climate Act) phases out high-GWP HFCs in newly manufactured or installed equipment. It directly covers retail food stand-alone refrigeration units (prohibited from Jan 9, 2025) and refrigerated food dispensing equipment (from Jan 1, 2027), and subjects owners of equipment containing 50 pounds or more of refrigerant to a Refrigerant Management Program with leak-control and recordkeeping duties.
businessImpact: When a cafe buys new reach-in coolers, ice machines, or refrigerated dispensing equipment for NY locations, it must specify Part 494-compliant low-GWP refrigerants or face purchase/installation prohibitions.
recommendedAction: Specify low-GWP/Part 494-compliant refrigerants in new equipment procurement and verify any unit with a 50 lb or greater charge enrolls in leak management. 采购新制冷设备时指定符合494部分的低GWP制冷剂。

regulationName: Air Pollution Control Code - Commercial Cook Stove & Char Broiler Emission Controls (Local Law 38 of 2015)
chineseTitle: 商用炉灶与炭烤设备排放控制（2015年第38号地方法）
englishTitle: Commercial Cook Stove & Char Broiler Emission Controls
agency: New York City Department of Environmental Protection (DEP)
applicabilityThreshold: Commercial cook stoves (any FSE operating one); char broilers cooking more than 875 lbs of meat per week. 任何使用商用炉灶的餐饮场所；每周烤制超过875磅肉类的炭烤设备。
keyRequirements: Food service establishments operating commercial cook stoves (Admin Code 24-149.5) must install emission control devices achieving at least 75% particulate reduction (existing stoves compliant by Jan 1, 2020; new stoves on startup), and commercial char broilers (Admin Code 24-149.4) cooking more than 875 pounds of meat per week must install certified emission controls.
chineseSummary: 纽约市《空气污染控制法》经2015年第38号地方法修订，要求餐饮场所为商用炉灶（行政法典§24-149.5）安装可实现至少75%颗粒物削减的排放控制装置。2016年5月6日前安装的炉灶须在2020年1月1日前达标，之后安装的须启用时即达标；每周烤制超过875磅肉类的炭烤设备（§24-149.4）须安装经认证的排放控制装置，控制装置须定期检查清洁并留存记录。
englishSummary: NYC's Air Pollution Control Code, amended by Local Law 38 of 2015 (Admin Code 24-149.5 for cook stoves and 24-149.4 for char broilers), requires food service establishments with commercial cook stoves to install emission control devices achieving at least 75% particulate-matter reduction. Cook stoves installed before May 6, 2016 had to comply by Jan 1, 2020; commercial char broilers cooking more than 875 lbs of meat per week need certified controls, with ongoing inspection and recordkeeping.
businessImpact: If a cafe operates a commercial cook stove or high-volume char broiler, it must install, certify, and maintain DEP-approved emission controls or face DEP violations.
recommendedAction: Audit kitchen cooking equipment for cook-stove/char-broiler triggers and install/recertify DEP-approved emission controls with a maintenance log. 核查厨房炉灶是否触发，安装并维护DEP认证排放控制装置。

regulationName: Trade Waste Removal License & Licensed Private Carter Requirement (Admin Code Title 16-B)
chineseTitle: 商业废物清运许可与持牌私营清运商要求
englishTitle: Trade Waste Removal License & Licensed Private Carter Requirement
agency: New York City Business Integrity Commission (BIC)
applicabilityThreshold: Every commercial establishment generating trade waste in NYC. 在纽约市产生商业垃圾的所有商业场所。
keyRequirements: Every commercial establishment must arrange removal of its trade waste and recyclables by a BIC-licensed private carter (or register as a self-hauler), and written carting contracts may not exceed two years; haulers themselves hold a BIC Trade Waste Removal License ($5,000, two-year term).
chineseSummary: 依据纽约市行政法典第16-B编（Local Law 42 of 1996设立BIC），所有商业场所必须由经商业诚信委员会（BIC）发牌的私营清运商清运其商业垃圾与可回收物，或自行登记为自运商。清运合同期限不得超过两年；清运商须持有BIC商业废物清运许可（费用5000美元、两年有效期）。该制度旨在防止私营清运行业的有组织犯罪与价格欺诈。
englishSummary: Under NYC Admin Code Title 16-B, every commercial establishment must have its trade waste and recyclables collected by a BIC-licensed private carter or register as a self-hauler, and carting contracts cannot exceed two years. Carters must hold a BIC Trade Waste Removal License ($5,000, two-year renewable term); the regime exists to curb organized crime and overcharging in the carting industry.
businessImpact: A cafe must contract only with a BIC-licensed carter, keep the carting agreement to two years or less, and retain the Customer Bill of Rights, or risk fines for using an unlicensed hauler.
recommendedAction: Verify your carter holds a current BIC license, keep contracts at two years max, and retain manifests/Customer Bill of Rights. 确认清运商持有有效BIC牌照，合同不超过两年并留存清运凭证。

regulationName: NYC Plumbing Code Section 1003.3.1 - Grease Interceptor Design & Installation Requirement
chineseTitle: 纽约市管道规范第1003.3.1节——隔油池设计与安装要求
englishTitle: Grease Interceptor Design & Installation Requirement
agency: New York City Department of Buildings (DOB); sizing per Department of Environmental Protection (DEP)
applicabilityThreshold: Food preparation areas with fixtures/equipment discharging fats, oils, or grease. 食品加工区内有油脂排放器具/设备的场所。
keyRequirements: A grease interceptor or automatic grease removal device must be installed to receive drainage from fixtures and equipment handling fats, oils, or grease in food preparation areas (Sec. 1003.3.1), designed and tested per PDI G101/ASME A112.14.3 or A112.14.4 (Sec. 1003.3.4) and sized per DEP rules.
chineseSummary: 纽约市《管道规范》第1003.3.1节要求，餐饮场所食品加工区内涉及油脂的器具和设备的排水必须经隔油池或自动除油装置处理；装置须符合PDI G101或ASME A112.14.3/A112.14.4标准（第1003.3.4节），并按DEP规则确定容量。该装置须由纽约市持牌总管道工安装，是新建或改建厨房在施工阶段必须满足的建筑/管道规范义务，与DEP的油脂排放禁令相互补充。
englishSummary: NYC Plumbing Code Section 1003.3.1 requires a grease interceptor or automatic grease removal device on drainage from fixtures and equipment handling fats, oils, or grease in food preparation areas; the device must be designed and tested per PDI G101 or ASME A112.14.3/A112.14.4 (Sec. 1003.3.4) and sized per DEP rules. It is a build-out/plumbing-code obligation enforced by DOB through plumbing plan approval and must be installed by a NYC-licensed master plumber, complementing DEP's FOG-discharge rules.
businessImpact: When building out or renovating a cafe kitchen, the operator must install a properly sized, code-conforming grease interceptor through a licensed master plumber to pass DOB plumbing inspection and open.
recommendedAction: At lease/build-out, engage a NYC-licensed master plumber to design and install a DEP-sized, code-conforming grease interceptor before opening. 装修阶段委托持牌总管道工设计安装合规隔油池。

regulationName: Single-Use Plastic Beverage Stirrer & Splash Stick Ban (Local Law 64 of 2021, Admin Code 16-401)
chineseTitle: 一次性塑料搅拌棒与防溅棒禁令（2021年第64号地方法）
englishTitle: Single-Use Plastic Beverage Stirrer & Splash Stick Ban
agency: New York City Department of Sanitation (DSNY)
applicabilityThreshold: All NYC food service establishments. 纽约市所有餐饮场所。
keyRequirements: Food service establishments may not provide any single-use plastic beverage stirrer or beverage splash stick (an outright ban, distinct from the straws-on-request rule), and must instead offer non-plastic compostable alternatives such as wood.
chineseSummary: 依据2021年第64号地方法（行政法典§16-401），自2021年11月1日起，纽约市餐饮场所不得提供任何一次性塑料饮料搅拌棒或防溅棒——这是彻底禁令，区别于塑料吸管仅可应要求提供的规定。场所须改用木质等非塑料可堆肥替代品。由DSNY执法，违者可处罚款（首次250美元，再犯最高500美元）。
englishSummary: Under Local Law 64 of 2021 (Admin Code 16-401), effective November 1, 2021, NYC food service establishments may not provide any single-use plastic beverage stirrer or beverage splash stick - an outright ban, distinct from the separate straws-on-request requirement of the same law. Operators must switch to non-plastic compostable alternatives such as wood, with DSNY enforcing via penalties ($250 first offense, up to $500 thereafter).
businessImpact: A cafe must remove all plastic stirrers and lid splash sticks from service and switch to wood/compostable versions, or face DSNY fines.
recommendedAction: Replace plastic stirrers and splash sticks with wood/compostable alternatives across all NYC stores immediately. 立即将塑料搅拌棒与防溅棒替换为木质/可堆肥替代品。

regulationName: Packaging Reduction and Recycling Infrastructure Act (S1464A / A1749A)
chineseTitle: 包装减量与回收基础设施法案（拟议EPR法案）
englishTitle: Packaging Reduction and Recycling Infrastructure Act (PRRIA)
agency: New York State Legislature / proposed for NYSDEC implementation
applicabilityThreshold: Producers of packaging with $5 million or more in annual gross revenue handling 2+ tons of packaging (if enacted). 年总收入达500万美元及以上且年处理包装达2吨的包装生产者（如获通过）。
keyRequirements: Would impose extended producer responsibility on companies with $5 million or more in gross revenue that sell or distribute packaging (and that handle 2 tons or more of packaging annually), requiring registration with a packaging reduction organization, source-reduction targets, and bans on listed packaging toxins.
chineseSummary: 《包装减量与回收基础设施法案》（PRRIA，S1464A/A1749A）是一项拟议的延伸生产者责任（EPR）法案，要求年总收入达500万美元及以上、销售或分销包装的企业（且年处理包装达2吨及以上）向包装减量组织注册并制定减量回收计划，设定逐年提高的减量目标，并禁止列明的包装毒性物质。该法案2025年5月28日获参议院通过但众议院未表决，2026年经近150项修订后仍在委员会审议，尚未生效。
englishSummary: PRRIA (S1464A/A1749A) is a proposed extended-producer-responsibility bill requiring companies with $5 million or more in gross revenue that sell or distribute packaging (and that handle 2 tons or more of packaging annually) to register with a packaging reduction organization, meet source-reduction targets, and stop using listed packaging toxins. It passed the Senate on May 28, 2025 but the Assembly did not vote; after nearly 150 amendments in 2026 it remains in committee and is not yet law.
businessImpact: If enacted, a multi-store cafe chain that packages or imports its own branded cups, lids, sleeves, and bags above the revenue threshold would face EPR registration, fees, and packaging-reduction mandates.
recommendedAction: Monitor PRRIA progress; inventory branded packaging and model EPR fee exposure so the chain can comply quickly if the bill passes. 跟踪法案进展，盘点自有品牌包装并预估EPR费用风险。

regulationName: Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law (ECL Article 27, Title 22)
chineseTitle: 食品捐赠与食物残渣回收法
englishTitle: Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law
agency: New York State Department of Environmental Conservation (NYSDEC)
applicabilityThreshold: Generators averaging 2 tons/week of food waste within 25 mi of an organics recycler (1 ton/50 mi from 2027); NYC exempt. 每周2吨且距有机回收设施25英里内（2027年起1吨/50英里）；纽约市豁免。
keyRequirements: Designated food scraps generators producing an annual average of 2 tons or more of food waste per week must donate edible food and recycle remaining food scraps if within 25 miles of an organics recycler; the threshold drops to 1 ton/week within 50 miles effective January 1, 2027.
chineseSummary: 纽约州《食品捐赠与食物残渣回收法》自2022年1月1日起生效，要求年均每周产生2吨及以上食物废弃物且位于有机回收设施25英里范围内的指定食物残渣生产者，须捐赠可食用食品并回收剩余食物残渣。自2027年1月1日起门槛降至每周1吨、范围扩大至50英里。纽约市因有自身的有机物分类法而被豁免，故该法主要适用于咖啡店在纽约州其他地区（非纽约市）的门店扩张。
englishSummary: New York's Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law (effective Jan 1, 2022) requires designated food scraps generators producing an annual average of 2 tons or more of food waste per week, and located within 25 miles of an organics recycler, to donate edible food and recycle remaining scraps. The threshold tightens to 1 ton/week within 50 miles on Jan 1, 2027. NYC is exempt because of its own commercial organics law, so this matters mainly for non-NYC New York State locations.
businessImpact: A high-volume cafe or roastery outside NYC that crosses the 2-ton/week food-waste threshold must arrange edible-food donation and organics recycling, a consideration as the chain expands across New York State.
recommendedAction: Estimate per-site weekly food-waste tonnage for non-NYC NY locations and pre-arrange donation/organics recycling before crossing the 2-ton (soon 1-ton) threshold. 评估纽约州非纽约市门店的每周食物废弃量并提前安排捐赠与回收。

regulationName: Local Law 97 of 2019 - Building Greenhouse Gas Emissions Limits (Climate Mobilization Act)
chineseTitle: 2019年第97号地方法——建筑温室气体排放限值
englishTitle: Building Greenhouse Gas Emissions Limits
agency: New York City Department of Buildings (DOB)
applicabilityThreshold: Buildings over 25,000 sq ft (owner-borne); tenants affected indirectly via lease. 面积超25000平方英尺的建筑（业主承担）；租户经租约间接受影响。
keyRequirements: Buildings over 25,000 square feet must meet annual greenhouse-gas emissions caps (first compliance period 2024-2029) and file annual emissions reports by May 1, with penalties up to $268 per metric ton of CO2e over the limit.
chineseSummary: 纽约市2019年第97号地方法（《气候动员法》的一部分）要求面积超过25000平方英尺的建筑自2024年起满足年度温室气体排放上限（首个合规期2024-2029年），业主须于每年5月1日前提交排放报告，超限部分每公吨二氧化碳当量最高罚款268美元。该义务主要落在建筑业主/房东身上，但租户的能耗会影响整栋楼的合规，房东可能通过租约将成本转嫁给咖啡店等租户。
englishSummary: NYC Local Law 97 (part of the Climate Mobilization Act) requires buildings over 25,000 sq ft to meet annual GHG emission caps starting in 2024 (first compliance period 2024-2029), with annual reports due May 1 and penalties up to $268 per metric ton of CO2e over the limit. The compliance duty falls on building owners/landlords, but tenant energy use affects whole-building compliance and landlords may pass costs to cafe tenants via lease provisions.
businessImpact: A cafe leasing space in a covered building is not the direct filer but may bear pass-through compliance costs and face landlord requirements to reduce its electricity and gas usage.
recommendedAction: Review lease clauses for LL97 cost pass-through and prioritize energy-efficient equipment to limit your share of building emissions. 审查租约中LL97成本转嫁条款，优先采用节能设备。

regulationName: New Jersey Statewide Mandatory Source Separation and Recycling Act
chineseTitle: 新泽西州强制源头分类与回收法
englishTitle: NJ Statewide Mandatory Source Separation and Recycling Act
agency: New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection (NJDEP), Division of Sustainable Waste Management; administered locally via county/municipal recycling coordinators
applicabilityThreshold: All commercial generators; specific materials and rules set by each municipality's ordinance / 适用于所有商业产废者，具体物料由各市条例规定
keyRequirements: All commercial and institutional generators must source-separate and recycle the specific recyclable materials designated in their municipality's recycling ordinance.
chineseSummary: 1987年通过的《强制源头分类与回收法》(N.J.S.A. 13:1E-99.11 及其后续条款) 要求全州居民、企业和机构对市政回收条例指定的材料进行源头分类回收。每个市政当局依据所属县的回收计划制定具体的强制回收条例，规定纸张、玻璃、金属、塑料、纸板等需分类的物料。咖啡店作为商业产废者，须按门店所在市的条例进行分类并保留回收记录。
englishSummary: Enacted in 1987 (N.J.S.A. 13:1E-99.11 et seq.), this foundational law requires every New Jersey resident, business, and institution to source-separate and recycle designated materials. Each municipality adopts its own mandatory recycling ordinance (based on the county plan) specifying which materials (e.g., paper/cardboard, glass, metal, plastics) must be separated. A café is a commercial generator and must comply with its host municipality's ordinance and keep recycling records.
businessImpact: Every NJ café location must set up source-separated recycling per its town's ordinance and may be cited by municipal/county inspectors for commingled or unrecycled designated materials.
recommendedAction: Pull each store municipality's recycling ordinance, set up designated-material separation, and assign a recycling point person / 调取各门店所在市的回收条例并落实分类。

regulationName: New Jersey Uniform Construction Code — Plumbing Subcode, Grease Interceptor Requirement
chineseTitle: 新泽西州统一建筑规范——隔油池要求
englishTitle: NJ Uniform Construction Code Plumbing Subcode — Grease Interceptor Requirement
agency: NJ Department of Community Affairs (Uniform Construction Code) with enforcement by local sewer utilities/authorities and municipal plumbing officials
applicabilityThreshold: All retail food establishments producing grease/oil-laden wastewater / 所有产生油脂废水的零售食品场所
keyRequirements: Food establishments producing grease- or oil-laden waste must install, size, and maintain grease interceptors per the State Plumbing Subcode so excessive FOG is not discharged to the sanitary sewer.
chineseSummary: 依据新泽西州《统一建筑规范》水暖分规 (N.J.A.C. 5:23 第3.15节，采纳国家标准管道规范)，所有产生油脂废水的零售食品场所必须安装并维护隔油池/隔油器，防止过量油脂 (FOG) 排入卫生下水道。各市/污水管理机构通常另行规定隔油池尺寸、清掏周期 (常为按季)、需由持证清运商出具清运单据并保存记录 (常需保存三年)。
englishSummary: Under the NJ Uniform Construction Code Plumbing Subcode (N.J.A.C. 5:23-3.15, adopting the National Standard Plumbing Code), all retail food establishments that produce grease- or oil-laden waste must install and properly maintain grease interceptors to keep excessive FOG out of the sanitary sewer. Local sewer authorities and municipal ordinances typically layer on sizing, pumping frequency (often quarterly), licensed-hauler manifests, and multi-year recordkeeping.
businessImpact: A café with espresso, dish, and food prep needs a correctly sized, regularly pumped grease interceptor; failure leads to plumbing-permit problems, sewer-authority fines, and shutdown risk.
recommendedAction: Verify each build-out includes a code-sized grease interceptor, schedule licensed-hauler pumping, and retain manifests / 确认每个门店装有符合规范的隔油池并按期由持证清运商清掏保存单据。

regulationName: Solid Waste Utility Control Act / A-901 Licensing for Waste Transporters
chineseTitle: 固体废物运输商 A-901 许可制度
englishTitle: A-901 Licensing for Solid/Hazardous Waste Transporters
agency: NJDEP Division of Sustainable Waste Management, with the NJ Office of the Attorney General and NJ State Police (background vetting)
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to the waste-hauling vendors a café hires (third-party transporters), not the café itself / 适用于咖啡店聘用的第三方清运商，而非咖啡店本身
keyRequirements: Companies that collect/transport solid waste generated by third parties must hold an NJDEP A-901 license and CPCN and register each vehicle for waste decals before hauling.
chineseSummary: 新泽西州要求商业从事废物运输/经纪的企业必须取得 NJDEP 的 A-901 许可证及《公共便利与必要性证书》(CPCN)，并为每辆运输车辆登记领取废物标贴。该制度通过背景审查 (含指纹、犯罪记录核查) 以杜绝非法倾倒和有组织犯罪。咖啡店本身通常无需持证，但其聘用的垃圾/食物残渣/油脂清运商必须持有有效 A-901 许可。
englishSummary: New Jersey requires any company commercially collecting or transporting third-party-generated solid waste to hold an NJDEP A-901 license and a Certificate of Public Convenience and Necessity (CPCN), plus register each vehicle for waste decals. The licensing scheme uses background vetting (disclosure statements, fingerprinting) to keep organized crime and illegal dumping out of the waste industry. A café does not need the license itself, but its trash, food-scrap, and grease haulers must be A-901 licensed.
businessImpact: Using an unlicensed hauler exposes the café to liability and service disruption; operators should verify each waste/grease/organics vendor holds a current A-901 license and decals.
recommendedAction: Require proof of A-901 license, CPCN, and current waste decals from every waste/grease/organics hauler before contracting / 签约前要求所有清运商提供有效 A-901 许可、CPCN 及车辆标贴。

regulationName: Recycling Enhancement Act (P.L. 2007, c.311) — Recycling Tax
chineseTitle: 回收促进法（回收税）
englishTitle: Recycling Enhancement Act — $3/ton Recycling Tax
agency: NJ Division of Taxation and NJDEP (recycling program funding)
applicabilityThreshold: Imposed at the disposal/transfer facility level; cost typically passed through to generators / 在处置/转运设施层面征收，成本通常转嫁给产废者
keyRequirements: A $3.00-per-ton recycling tax is imposed on solid waste accepted for disposal/transfer at NJ facilities (and on out-of-state-bound waste), funding state and municipal recycling programs.
chineseSummary: 《回收促进法》(P.L. 2007, c.311) 自2008年4月1日起对在新泽西州固废设施处置/转运的每吨固体废物征收3美元回收税，运往州外处置的废物亦适用。该税由设施运营商或废物清运商缴纳，所得用于市政回收补贴、县级固废规划及全州回收项目。咖啡店通常通过清运合同间接承担该成本，并应注意其废物计费中是否含此项税费。
englishSummary: The Recycling Enhancement Act (P.L. 2007, c.311) imposes a $3.00-per-ton tax, effective April 1, 2008, on solid waste accepted for disposal or transfer at NJ solid waste facilities (and on waste transported out of state). The tax is paid by facility operators or collectors and funds municipal recycling grants, county solid-waste planning, and the state recycling program. A café bears this cost indirectly through its waste-collection contract.
businessImpact: The recycling tax is typically passed through in a café's waste-disposal invoices, so increasing recycling/organics diversion can lower the taxable disposal tonnage and cost.
recommendedAction: Review waste invoices for pass-through recycling tax and maximize recycling/organics diversion to cut disposal tonnage / 审查清运账单中的回收税并通过提高分类回收率降低处置吨数。

regulationName: New Jersey Food Waste Recycling and Food Waste-to-Energy Production Law (P.L. 2020, c.24)
chineseTitle: 新泽西州食物垃圾回收法（大型产废者）
englishTitle: NJ Food Waste Recycling Law — Large Food Waste Generators (P.L. 2020 c.24)
applicabilityThreshold: ≥52 tons/year food waste per establishment AND within 25 road miles of an authorized facility / 单店年食物垃圾≥52吨且距核准设施25英里行车范围内
keyRequirements: Each establishment generating an average of 52+ tons/year of food waste and located within 25 road miles of an authorized food-waste recycling facility must source-separate its food waste and send it to that facility.
chineseSummary: 《食物垃圾回收与食物垃圾制能法》(P.L. 2020, c.24；N.J.S.A. 13:1E-99.122 及后续条款，实施细则 N.J.A.C. 7:26K) 自2021年10月14日生效。凡单一场所年均产生52吨及以上食物垃圾、且位于经核准的食物垃圾回收设施25英里行车范围内者，须将食物垃圾源头分类并送往该设施。门槛按单店计算；多数单体咖啡店年食物垃圾远低于52吨，但中央厨房、烘焙工厂或大型旗舰店可能触发。
englishSummary: Effective October 14, 2021, this law (N.J.S.A. 13:1E-99.122 et seq.; rules at N.J.A.C. 7:26K) requires any establishment that generates an average of 52 or more tons of food waste per year and sits within 25 road miles of an authorized food-waste recycling facility to source-separate its food waste and send it to that facility. The 52-ton threshold is measured per individual location. Most single cafés fall well under 52 tons, but a central kitchen, commissary, or high-volume flagship could trigger it.
businessImpact: A standalone café rarely hits 52 tons/year, but any NJ commissary or central production kitchen must track food-waste tonnage and arrange organics recycling if over the threshold and within 25 miles of a facility.
recommendedAction: Estimate annual food-waste tonnage per NJ location; for any site near/over 52 tons within 25 miles of a facility, set up organics hauling / 按门店估算年食物垃圾吨数，对临近或超过52吨者安排有机垃圾清运。

regulationName: New Jersey Plastic Pollution Reduction Act — Carryout Bag Ban & Plastic Straw-on-Request (P.L. 2020, c.117)
chineseTitle: 新泽西州塑料污染减量法——购物袋禁令与塑料吸管按需提供
englishTitle: NJ Plastic Pollution Reduction Act — Bag Ban & Straw-on-Request (P.L. 2020 c.117)
agency: NJDEP, with municipalities, County Environmental Health Act (CEHA) agencies, and NJ Department of Health
applicabilityThreshold: All food service businesses (straw-on-request and plastic-bag ban); paper-bag ban only for grocery stores ≥2,500 sq ft / 所有食品服务企业适用吸管与塑料袋规定；纸袋禁令仅适用≥2,500平方英尺杂货店
keyRequirements: Food service businesses may not provide single-use plastic carryout bags and may provide single-use plastic straws only upon customer request.
chineseSummary: 《塑料污染减量法》(P.L. 2020, c.117) 自2022年5月4日起禁止食品服务企业提供一次性塑料购物袋；面积≥2,500平方英尺的杂货店连纸袋也禁止 (只能提供可重复使用袋)。自2021年11月4日起，塑料吸管只能在顾客要求时提供。违规第一次警告，其后每日罚款最高1,000至5,000美元。NJDEP、市政当局、CEHA及卫生部门负责执法。
englishSummary: Effective May 4, 2022, the Plastic Pollution Reduction Act (P.L. 2020, c.117) bars food service businesses from providing single-use plastic carryout bags; grocery stores of 2,500+ sq ft also cannot provide paper bags (reusable only). Since November 4, 2021, single-use plastic straws may be provided only on customer request. Enforcement runs through NJDEP, municipalities, CEHA, and the Department of Health, with escalating per-day penalties (warning, then up to $1,000, then up to $5,000).
businessImpact: Every NJ café must drop plastic carryout bags, switch to compliant reusable/paper bags, and train staff to give plastic straws only when asked, or face escalating per-day fines.
recommendedAction: Eliminate single-use plastic bags chainwide, source compliant bags, and post straw-on-request signage with staff training / 全面停用一次性塑料袋，改用合规袋并培训员工吸管按需提供。

regulationName: New Jersey Polystyrene Foam Food Service Product Ban (P.L. 2020, c.117) & 2024 NJDEP Exemption Extension
chineseTitle: 新泽西州聚苯乙烯泡沫餐具禁令及2024年NJDEP豁免延期
englishTitle: NJ Polystyrene Foam Foodware Ban & 2024 NJDEP Exemption Extension
agency: NJDEP, with municipalities/CEHA and NJ Department of Health
applicabilityThreshold: All food service businesses; <$500,000 gross income may seek waiver; narrow exemptions extended to 2025-05-04 / 所有食品服务企业适用；总收入低于50万美元可申请豁免；个别豁免延至2025-05-04
keyRequirements: Food service businesses may not sell or provide polystyrene foam food service products (cups, plates, clamshells, trays, utensils), subject to limited NJDEP-extended exemptions and a small-business waiver.
chineseSummary: 同属《塑料污染减量法》(P.L. 2020, c.117)，自2022年5月4日起禁止销售或提供聚苯乙烯泡沫 (发泡塑料) 餐具，包括杯、盘、贝壳式餐盒、托盘和餐具。2024年2月16日，NJDEP将部分豁免 (生鲜肉鱼托盘及厂家预包装泡沫制品) 延期至2025年5月4日；同时2024年5月4日起加禁长柄泡沫汤匙和2盎司及以下小杯。年总收入低于50万美元的小企业在无合理替代品时可申请豁免。
englishSummary: Also under the Plastic Pollution Reduction Act (P.L. 2020, c.117), effective May 4, 2022, food service businesses may not sell or provide polystyrene foam food service products (cups, plates, clamshells, trays, utensils). On February 16, 2024, NJDEP extended limited exemptions (raw meat/fish trays and manufacturer-pre-packaged foam items) to May 4, 2025, while foam soda spoons and <=2 oz portion cups became banned May 4, 2024. A business with under $500,000 gross annual income may seek a waiver where no affordable alternative exists.
businessImpact: Cafés must use non-foam cups, lids, trays, and containers; only narrow extended exemptions remain, so foam hot-cups and portion cups are out and substitutes must be sourced.
recommendedAction: Confirm no polystyrene foam in cups/lids/portion cups/containers chainwide; document any small-business waiver eligibility / 确认全链门店杯盖、小杯、容器均不含发泡塑料，并保留小企业豁免资格证明。

regulationName: New Jersey Prohibition on Hydrofluorocarbons in Certain Products and Equipment (P.L. 2019, c.507 / A5583)
chineseTitle: 新泽西州特定设备氢氟碳化物（HFC）禁令
englishTitle: NJ Prohibition on Hydrofluorocarbons in Certain Equipment (P.L. 2019 c.507)
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to new equipment purchases/installations by end-use category; existing equipment grandfathered unless retrofitted / 适用于按用途分类的新设备购置/安装；现有设备除改造外不受影响
keyRequirements: Sale, lease, rent, or installation of new equipment using prohibited high-GWP hydrofluorocarbons is banned by end-use category on staggered dates (supermarket/stand-alone refrigeration from July 1, 2020; vending machines from Jan 1, 2022).
chineseSummary: 《特定产品和设备氢氟碳化物禁令法》(P.L. 2019, c.507；A5583) 按设备类别分阶段禁止销售、租赁或安装使用高全球变暖潜值 HFC 制冷剂的新设备：超市制冷系统、远程冷凝机组和独立制冷机组自2020年7月1日起；冷藏食品加工与分配设备自2021年1月1日起；自动售货机自2022年1月1日起。NJDEP负责执法。现有设备可继续使用 (除非改造)，故主要影响新购或更换的冷藏/冰沙/展示柜设备。
englishSummary: P.L. 2019, c.507 (A5583) bans the sale, lease, rent, or installation of new equipment using prohibited high-GWP hydrofluorocarbon refrigerants, phased by end-use: supermarket/remote-condensing/stand-alone refrigeration from July 1, 2020; refrigerated food processing and dispensing equipment from Jan 1, 2021; vending machines from Jan 1, 2022. NJDEP enforces it. Existing equipment may keep running unless retrofitted, so the impact is on newly purchased or replaced refrigeration/display/dispensing units.
businessImpact: When a NJ café buys or replaces refrigerators, display cases, cold-brew/dispensing units, or vending machines, the equipment must use compliant non-prohibited refrigerants.
recommendedAction: Specify compliant low-GWP refrigerants in all new NJ refrigeration/dispensing equipment purchases and keep manufacturer compliance documentation / 新购冷藏与分配设备须采用合规低GWP制冷剂并留存厂家合规证明。

regulationName: New Jersey Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NJPDES) — Industrial Wastewater & Sewer Connection Permitting (N.J.A.C. 7:14A)
chineseTitle: 新泽西州污染物排放消除系统（NJPDES）——工业废水与下水道接入许可
englishTitle: NJPDES Industrial Wastewater & Sewer Connection Permitting (N.J.A.C. 7:14A)
applicabilityThreshold: Direct dischargers / sewer-connection builders need NJPDES permits; sewer-connected cafés governed by local POTW pretreatment rules / 直排或建设下水道接入者需NJPDES许可；接入市政下水道的咖啡店受当地POTW预处理规则管辖
keyRequirements: No person may discharge pollutants to waters of the State or build/operate wastewater collection or sewer-connection facilities except in conformance with an NJPDES permit and the local sewerage authority's pretreatment rules.
chineseSummary: 依据《新泽西州水污染控制法》及实施规则 N.J.A.C. 7:14A，未经 NJPDES 许可不得向州水体排放污染物，也不得建设或运营废水收集/下水道接入设施。普通咖啡店排入市政卫生下水道，由当地污水管理机构 (POTW) 的预处理规则管辖 (含 FOG、pH、温度等限值)，通常无需单独 NJPDES 工业许可；但中央厨房或就地排放/排入州水体者可能需要个别许可。
englishSummary: Under the NJ Water Pollution Control Act and N.J.A.C. 7:14A, no one may discharge pollutants to waters of the State or build/operate wastewater-collection or sewer-connection facilities except under an NJPDES permit. A typical café discharging to a municipal sanitary sewer is governed by the local sewerage authority's (POTW) pretreatment rules (FOG, pH, temperature limits) and usually needs no separate NJPDES industrial permit, but a commissary or any site discharging to ground/surface waters may require an individual permit.
businessImpact: A café on municipal sewer must meet its sewerage authority's pretreatment limits (especially FOG, pH), while any non-standard discharge or commissary build-out may need NJDEP permitting.
recommendedAction: Confirm each site discharges only to municipal sewer within pretreatment limits; flag any direct or ground discharge for NJPDES permit review / 确认各门店仅在预处理限值内排入市政下水道，对任何直排或地面排放进行NJPDES许可评估。

regulationName: Basic Industrial Stormwater General Permit (NJPDES Permit No. 5G2)
chineseTitle: 新泽西州基础工业雨水通用许可（5G2）
englishTitle: NJ Basic Industrial Stormwater General Permit (5G2)
agency: NJDEP Bureau of NJPDES Stormwater Permitting
applicabilityThreshold: Covered industrial categories with stormwater exposure (e.g., warehousing, fleet, manufacturing); not typical retail storefronts / 适用有雨水暴露的特定工业类别（仓储、车队、制造等），普通零售门店一般不适用
keyRequirements: Facilities in covered industrial categories that expose materials/activities to stormwater must obtain coverage under the Basic Industrial Stormwater General Permit (5G2), prepare a Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plan (SPPP), and conduct monitoring.
chineseSummary: NJDEP 的基础工业雨水通用许可 (5G2，属 N.J.A.C. 7:14A 框架) 要求特定工业类别 (如运输、仓储、制造、回收设施) 凡有材料或活动暴露于雨水者，须获许可覆盖、编制并执行《雨水污染防治计划》(SPPP) 并开展监测。普通零售咖啡店一般不属受管工业类别，无需该许可；但若设有中央仓储/配送中心或车队维护场地暴露于雨水，则可能触发。
englishSummary: NJDEP's Basic Industrial Stormwater General Permit (5G2, under N.J.A.C. 7:14A) requires facilities in covered industrial categories (e.g., transportation, warehousing, manufacturing, recycling) that expose materials or activities to stormwater to obtain permit coverage, prepare and implement a Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plan (SPPP), and conduct monitoring. A typical retail café is not a regulated industrial category, but a central warehouse/distribution center or fleet-maintenance yard with outdoor exposure could trigger coverage.
businessImpact: Retail café storefronts generally fall outside this permit, but a NJ distribution hub, roasting plant, or fleet yard with outdoor material exposure may need 5G2 coverage and an SPPP.
recommendedAction: Assess whether any NJ warehouse/roastery/fleet site has outdoor industrial exposure requiring 5G2 coverage and an SPPP / 评估新泽西仓储/烘焙/车队场地是否存在户外工业暴露需办理5G2许可及SPPP。

regulationName: New Jersey Packaging Product Stewardship Act (S208 / S3398, 2024–2025 Session) — Proposed Packaging EPR
chineseTitle: 新泽西州包装产品管理法案（拟议的包装生产者责任延伸制度）
englishTitle: NJ Packaging Product Stewardship Act (Proposed Packaging EPR, S208/S3398)
applicabilityThreshold: Would apply to producers/brand owners of packaging sold in NJ (thresholds TBD in final bill) / 将适用于在新泽西销售包装的生产者/品牌方（具体门槛待最终法案确定）
keyRequirements: Would require producers of packaging products sold in New Jersey to join a producer responsibility organization and implement approved stewardship plans to reduce, redesign, and fund recycling of packaging.
chineseSummary: 拟议中的《包装产品管理法》(S208，并有同类法案 S3398/A5009) 将在新泽西建立全州包装生产者责任延伸 (EPR) 制度，要求在州内销售包装产品的生产者制定并实施经批准的包装管理计划，减少包装用量、改进可回收设计并为回收系统提供资金。该法案曾于2025年2月经参议院环境与能源委员会通过，但在2024-2025会期未获最终立法通过；预计后续会期将以同类法案重新提出。若实施将影响连锁品牌的杯、盖、包装等一次性包装的设计与收费义务。
englishSummary: The proposed Packaging Product Stewardship Act (S208, with companion bills S3398/A5009) would create a statewide packaging Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) program in New Jersey, requiring producers of packaging sold in the state to develop and implement approved stewardship plans that reduce packaging, improve recyclability, and fund recycling. S208 advanced out of the Senate Environment and Energy Committee in February 2025 but was not enacted by the close of the 2024–2025 session; a companion measure is expected to be reintroduced. If passed, it would affect how chains design and pay for single-use cups, lids, and packaging.
businessImpact: If enacted, a coffee chain selling packaged products/cups in NJ could face producer fees, recyclability standards, and reporting obligations for its packaging.
recommendedAction: Monitor S208/S3398 progress and inventory NJ packaging (cups, lids, sleeves) for likely future EPR fees and recyclability rules / 跟踪S208/S3398进展，盘点新泽西包装以备未来EPR收费与可回收要求。

regulationName: SB 1383 — Short-Lived Climate Pollutants: Organic Waste Reduction (Mandatory Commercial Organics Recycling)
chineseTitle: SB 1383 商业有机废弃物强制分类与回收法
englishTitle: SB 1383 Mandatory Commercial Organic Waste Recycling
agency: California Department of Resources Recycling and Recovery (CalRecycle)
applicabilityThreshold: All commercial waste generators in California (no size minimum); edible-food recovery tiers apply only to large generators. / 适用于加州所有商业废弃物产生者（无规模下限）。
keyRequirements: All businesses must subscribe to organics and recycling collection service (or self-haul), provide correctly color-coded green/blue/grey containers in customer and back-of-house areas, sort food scraps and recyclables out of trash, and train staff annually.
chineseSummary: SB 1383 是加州减少短寿命气候污染物战略的核心法规，自2022年1月1日起要求所有企业（含咖啡店）将有机废弃物（食物残渣等）和可回收物与垃圾分开收集。企业须订购有机物收集服务、配置规定颜色的分类容器、每年培训员工并配合稽查；自2024年1月1日起地方政府可对违规者处以罚款。大型食物产生者还须按层级（Tier 2 自2024年起）将多余可食用食物捐赠给食物回收机构。
englishSummary: SB 1383 is the cornerstone of California's short-lived climate pollutant strategy and, since January 1, 2022, requires every business (cafés included) to separate organic waste and recyclables from trash via subscribed collection or self-haul. Operators must supply color-coded bins, train staff yearly, and permit jurisdiction inspections; local enforcement and fines became available January 1, 2024. Large edible-food generators must also contract with a food recovery organization (Tier 2 generators by January 1, 2024).
businessImpact: Every Luckin café in California must arrange compliant green-bin organics + recycling collection and staff sorting, or face local fines starting in 2024.
recommendedAction: Confirm each store's hauler provides three-stream (organics/recycling/trash) service, place compliant color-coded bins, and run annual staff sorting training. / 确认每店有机物+回收三分类收集并每年培训员工。

regulationName: AB 1276 — Single-Use Foodware Accessories and Standard Condiments
chineseTitle: AB 1276 一次性餐具配件与标准调味品（按需提供）法
englishTitle: AB 1276 Single-Use Foodware Accessories Available by Request Only
agency: Local enforcement agency designated by each city/county (statute in CA Public Resources Code)
applicabilityThreshold: All food facilities serving prepared food in California (no size minimum). / 适用于加州所有提供即食食品的食品场所（无规模下限）。
keyRequirements: Single-use foodware accessories (utensils, straws, stirrers, chopsticks, condiment cups/packets), including compostable and paper ones, may only be provided upon customer request, by self-serve dispenser, or (at drive-throughs) when staff explicitly ask — never bundled or automatically included.
chineseSummary: AB 1276 自2022年1月1日起要求加州所有食品场所及外卖平台仅在顾客主动要求时、或通过自助取用方式提供一次性餐具配件和标准调味品，且不得将其捆绑或默认附送（含可堆肥、纸质、竹制等材质）。免下车通道可由员工口头询问后提供。第一、二次违规为警告，之后每日罚款25美元（年度上限300美元），城市/县自2024年起可执法。
englishSummary: Since January 1, 2022, AB 1276 requires all California food facilities and third-party delivery platforms to provide single-use foodware accessories and standard condiments only on request or via self-serve dispenser, never bundled or by default (covers compostable, paper, bamboo, etc.). Drive-throughs may have staff verbally ask. First two violations are notices; later violations are infractions at $25/day up to $300/year, with city/county enforcement available from 2024.
businessImpact: Luckin counters and delivery menus must default to no straw/stirrer/utensil unless the customer asks, requiring POS and app changes plus self-serve dispenser setup.
recommendedAction: Reconfigure POS/app and in-store dispensers so accessories are request-only; train baristas not to auto-bundle straws/stirrers. / 调整收银与App及取用台为按需提供，培训员工不默认附送。

regulationName: SB 54 — Plastic Pollution Prevention and Packaging Producer Responsibility Act
chineseTitle: SB 54 塑料污染防治与包装生产者责任法（EPR）
englishTitle: SB 54 Packaging Extended Producer Responsibility Act
agency: California Department of Resources Recycling and Recovery (CalRecycle); Circular Action Alliance (PRO)
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to 'producers' (brand owners/importers/distributors) of covered packaging and plastic foodware; small-producer revenue exemptions exist. / 适用于受管包装与塑料餐具的“生产者”，有小型生产者营收豁免。
keyRequirements: Producers of covered single-use packaging and plastic single-use food service ware (including cups, lids, and other foodware) must register with CalRecycle's approved Producer Responsibility Organization (Circular Action Alliance) by June 1, 2026, report supply data, pay EPR fees, and meet statewide source-reduction, recyclability, and recycling-rate targets through 2032.
chineseSummary: SB 54 是美国最全面的包装生产者延伸责任（EPR）法，2022年签署，永久法规于2026年5月1日生效，生产者须在2026年6月1日前注册。法规要求一次性包装和塑料一次性餐具（含杯、盖等）的“生产者”加入获批的生产者责任组织（Circular Action Alliance）、报送供应数据并缴费，并实现到2032年减塑25%、所有受管材料可回收或可堆肥等目标。若咖啡连锁自有品牌包装的供应链将其定义为“生产者”，则需直接承担注册与缴费义务。
englishSummary: SB 54 is the most comprehensive packaging EPR law in the U.S.; signed in 2022, its permanent regulations took effect May 1, 2026, with producer registration due June 1, 2026. Obligated 'producers' of single-use packaging and plastic single-use food service ware (cups, lids, etc.) must join the approved Producer Responsibility Organization (Circular Action Alliance), report supply data, pay fees, and meet 2027–2032 source-reduction, recyclability, and recycling-rate targets. A café chain can be pulled in as a producer where it is the brand owner/first distributor of its own-label packaging.
businessImpact: If Luckin is the brand owner of its own cups, lids, sleeves and packaging sold in California, it may be a 'producer' owing SB 54 registration, data reporting, and EPR fees.
recommendedAction: Determine producer status for Luckin-branded packaging; if obligated, register with Circular Action Alliance and begin supply-data reporting. / 评估自有品牌包装的生产者身份，如适用须向CAA注册并报送数据。

regulationName: SB 54 Expanded Polystyrene (EPS) Foam Food Service Ware Prohibition
chineseTitle: 加州发泡聚苯乙烯（EPS泡沫）餐具销售禁令（SB 54项下）
englishTitle: California EPS Foam Foodware Sale/Distribution Ban (under SB 54)
agency: California Department of Resources Recycling and Recovery (CalRecycle); CA Attorney General (enforcement)
applicabilityThreshold: Statewide ban on EPS foam foodware sale/distribution/import; affects all food businesses using such ware. / 全州禁止EPS泡沫餐具销售/分销/进口，影响所有使用此类餐具的食品企业。
keyRequirements: Because EPS producers failed to demonstrate a 25% recycling rate, expanded polystyrene foam food service ware (cups, clamshells, plates, bowls, trays, etc.) may no longer be sold, distributed, or imported in California, so food businesses must source non-foam alternatives.
chineseSummary: 根据 SB 54，发泡聚苯乙烯（EPS，俗称泡沫塑料/Styrofoam）餐具生产者须在2025年1月1日前证明达到25%回收率；因未达标，CalRecycle 认定自2025年1月1日起禁止在加州销售、分销或进口EPS泡沫餐具（杯、餐盒、盘、碗、托盘等）。2025年12月加州总检察长发布执法提示，违规可处每日每项最高5万美元行政罚款及不正当竞争法下民事处罚。咖啡店须改用非泡沫材质杯具与外带容器。
englishSummary: Under SB 54, EPS foam producers had to prove a 25% recycling rate by January 1, 2025; they failed, so CalRecycle confirmed a statewide ban on selling, distributing, or importing EPS foam food service ware (cups, clamshells, plates, bowls, trays) effective January 1, 2025. The CA Attorney General issued a December 2025 enforcement advisory warning of administrative penalties up to $50,000/day per violation plus unfair-competition civil penalties. Cafés must use non-foam cups and takeout containers.
businessImpact: Luckin cannot purchase or use EPS foam cups/containers in California and must switch to compliant paper, PET, or compostable foodware.
recommendedAction: Audit cup/container SKUs to eliminate any EPS foam items and confirm suppliers ship only non-foam alternatives to CA stores. / 核查并淘汰所有EPS泡沫杯具/容器，确保供应商对加州门店仅供应非泡沫替代品。

regulationName: AB 1200 — Plant-Based Food Packaging: PFAS Prohibition
chineseTitle: AB 1200 植物纤维食品包装PFAS（全氟化合物）禁令
englishTitle: AB 1200 PFAS Ban in Plant-Fiber Food Packaging
agency: California Department of Toxic Substances Control / CA Attorney General (enforcement); statute in Health & Safety Code
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to anyone selling/distributing covered plant-fiber food packaging in CA; obligation falls on manufacturers/distributors but constrains what cafés may use. / 适用于在加州销售/分销受管植物纤维食品包装者，约束咖啡店可采购的包装。
keyRequirements: Since January 1, 2023, it is unlawful to sell or distribute food packaging made substantially of paper, paperboard, or other plant fibers (e.g., molded-fiber clamshells, paper wrappers, cups, bags) that contains intentionally added PFAS or total organic fluorine at or above 100 ppm.
chineseSummary: AB 1200 自2023年1月1日起禁止在加州销售或分销主要由纸、纸板或其他植物纤维制成、且含有故意添加的PFAS或总有机氟≥100 ppm的食品包装（如模塑纤维餐盒、纸杯、纸袋、包装纸）。制造商须使用“毒性最低的替代品”。加州总检察长2023年10月发布执法提示。咖啡店应要求供应商提供“无PFAS（PFAS-free）”的纸质/纤维餐具与外带包装合规证明。
englishSummary: Effective January 1, 2023, AB 1200 prohibits selling or distributing food packaging made substantially of paper, paperboard, or other plant fibers that contains intentionally added PFAS or ≥100 ppm total organic fluorine (e.g., molded-fiber clamshells, paper cups, bags, wrappers). Manufacturers must use the 'least toxic alternative.' The CA Attorney General issued an enforcement advisory in October 2023. Cafés should obtain PFAS-free certifications for fiber-based foodware.
businessImpact: Luckin's paper cups, sleeves, bags, and fiber takeout containers used in California must be certified PFAS-free or they cannot be lawfully supplied.
recommendedAction: Collect PFAS-free / total-organic-fluorine compliance documentation from all fiber-foodware suppliers serving CA stores. / 向所有纤维餐具供应商索取无PFAS合规文件。

regulationName: SB 1206 — Hydrofluorocarbons: Prohibition on High-GWP Bulk HFC Sales
chineseTitle: SB 1206 高全球变暖潜值（GWP）散装HFC制冷剂销售禁令
englishTitle: SB 1206 High-GWP Bulk HFC Refrigerant Sales Phase-Down
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to sellers/distributors of bulk HFCs statewide; indirectly constrains all equipment owners needing service. / 适用于全州散装HFC销售/分销方，间接约束所有需维修设备的业主。
keyRequirements: Bulk HFC refrigerants (and blends) above set GWP limits may not be sold or distributed in California — GWP cap of 2,200 from January 1, 2025 (phasing out R-404A/R-507), dropping to 1,500 in 2030 (affecting R-410A) and 750 in 2033 — with virgin high-GWP refrigerant restricted and reclaimed refrigerant favored for servicing.
chineseSummary: SB 1206 由 CARB 执行，分阶段禁止在加州销售或分销超过规定GWP上限的散装HFC制冷剂及混合物：自2025年1月1日GWP上限为2,200（淘汰R-404A、R-507），2030年降至1,500（影响R-410A），2033年降至750。现有设备维修须优先使用回收再生制冷剂。咖啡店的冷藏/冷冻及空调设备在未来更换或大修制冷剂时，将受可用制冷剂种类与成本上升的影响。
englishSummary: Administered by CARB, SB 1206 phases down the sale/distribution of bulk HFC refrigerants and blends above set GWP limits in California: a 2,200 GWP cap from January 1, 2025 (phasing out R-404A/R-507), 1,500 from 2030 (affecting R-410A), and 750 from 2033. Servicing of existing equipment increasingly relies on reclaimed refrigerant. Café refrigeration, freezers, and HVAC will face narrower refrigerant choices and higher service costs at replacement/overhaul.
businessImpact: When Luckin's CA refrigeration or HVAC equipment needs recharging or replacement, only lower-GWP or reclaimed refrigerants will be legally available, raising service costs and equipment-spec requirements.
recommendedAction: Inventory refrigerants in CA store equipment and plan transitions to low-GWP/reclaimed refrigerants at next service or equipment refresh. / 盘点门店设备制冷剂，规划向低GWP/再生制冷剂过渡。

regulationName: Refrigerant Management Program (RMP), Title 17 CCR §§ 95380–95398
chineseTitle: 加州制冷剂管理计划（RMP，CARB，第17编）
englishTitle: CARB Refrigerant Management Program (RMP)
applicabilityThreshold: >50 lbs high-GWP refrigerant in the largest single system per facility (registration); 200+ lbs triggers annual reporting. / 单一最大系统>50磅高GWP需注册，≥200磅需年报。
keyRequirements: Owners/operators of stationary non-residential refrigeration systems with more than 50 pounds of high-GWP refrigerant in the largest system must register with CARB, perform periodic leak inspections, repair leaks, keep service records on-site, and (at 200+ lbs) file annual reports by March 1.
chineseSummary: 加州制冷剂管理计划（Title 17 CCR §95380–95398，由 CARB 执行）要求：任何固定式非住宅制冷系统中最大单一系统充注量超过50磅高GWP制冷剂的业主/运营者，须向 CARB 注册、定期进行泄漏检查与维修、现场保存维修记录；充注量达200磅及以上者须于每年3月1日前提交年度报告。多数小型咖啡店设备充注量低于50磅而无需注册，但大型中央制冷或大型设备门店可能触发义务。
englishSummary: California's RMP (Title 17 CCR §§ 95380–95398, enforced by CARB) requires owners/operators of stationary, non-residential refrigeration systems whose largest single system holds more than 50 pounds of high-GWP refrigerant to register with CARB, perform periodic leak inspections and repairs, keep on-site service records, and—at 200+ lbs—submit annual reports by March 1. Most small cafés stay below the 50-lb threshold and are exempt, but stores with large central refrigeration may be covered.
businessImpact: A Luckin store with a large central refrigeration system over 50 lbs of high-GWP refrigerant must register, log leak inspections, and keep records; smaller stores are typically exempt.
recommendedAction: Check the largest refrigerant charge per store; register and log inspections only if any single system exceeds 50 lbs high-GWP refrigerant. / 核查各店最大单系统充注量，超50磅高GWP才需注册并记录检查。

regulationName: Fats, Oils, and Grease (FOG) Control Program & California Plumbing Code Grease Interceptor Requirements
chineseTitle: 加州油脂（FOG）控制计划与加州管道规范隔油池要求
englishTitle: California FOG Control Program & Plumbing Code Grease Interceptor Rules
agency: State Water Resources Control Board / Regional Water Quality Control Boards (via local sewer agencies); California Building Standards Commission (Plumbing Code)
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to food service establishments where the local AHJ determines FOG pretreatment is required (varies by jurisdiction). / 适用于当地主管机构认定需油脂预处理的食品服务场所（因辖区而异）。
keyRequirements: Food service establishments determined by the local Authority Having Jurisdiction to need pretreatment must install, operate, and maintain an approved, properly sized grease interceptor per the California Plumbing Code, and clean it as needed (commonly when grease/solids reach 25% of capacity, at minimum periodic intervals) to prevent fats/oils/grease from causing sanitary sewer overflows.
chineseSummary: 加州州水资源控制委员会通过各区域水质控制委员会的废水排放要求，强制下水道机构建立并执行油脂（FOG）控制计划，以防止油脂导致污水溢流（SSO）。加州管道规范（California Plumbing Code）规定：经当地主管机构（AHJ）认定需预处理的食品服务场所，须安装、运行并维护经批准且规格适当的隔油池/隔油器，并按需清掏（通常浮油+沉淀物达容量25%即须清掏）。具体安装与清掏频率由当地下水道机构决定。
englishSummary: The State Water Resources Control Board, through Regional Water Board waste discharge requirements and the statewide Sanitary Sewer Systems General Order, mandates that sewer agencies adopt and enforce FOG control programs to prevent grease-caused sanitary sewer overflows. The California Plumbing Code requires food service establishments that the local Authority Having Jurisdiction determines need pretreatment to install, operate, and maintain an approved, properly sized grease interceptor, cleaning it as needed (commonly at the 25%-capacity rule). Exact sizing, permitting, and pump-out frequency are set by the local sewer agency.
businessImpact: Any Luckin store with espresso/milk/food prep generating grease may be required by its local sewer agency to install and routinely service a grease interceptor, with discharge permits and cleaning records.
recommendedAction: Confirm grease-interceptor requirements with each store's local sewer/water agency and set a documented pump-out schedule. / 向各店当地下水道机构确认隔油池要求并建立清掏记录。

regulationName: AB 793 — Recycling: Plastic Beverage Containers: Minimum Recycled Content
chineseTitle: AB 793 塑料饮料容器最低再生含量法
englishTitle: AB 793 Minimum Postconsumer Recycled Content for Plastic Beverage Containers
agency: California Department of Resources Recycling and Recovery (CalRecycle)
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to beverage manufacturers of CRV plastic beverage containers; not freshly poured in-cup drinks. / 适用于CRV塑料饮料容器的制造商，不含现做杯装饮品。
keyRequirements: Beverage manufacturers must ensure plastic (PET/HDPE) beverage containers subject to California Redemption Value contain minimum postconsumer recycled content rising from 15% (2022) to 25% (2025) and 50% (2030), reporting annually to CalRecycle.
chineseSummary: AB 793 要求纳入加州回收价值（CRV）的塑料饮料容器（PET/HDPE）含有最低消费后再生塑料含量，比例从2022年1月1日的15%，提升至2025年的25%，再到2030年的50%，并须向 CalRecycle 年度报告。义务主体为饮料制造商。若 Luckin 在加州销售自有品牌瓶装/塑料杯装即饮饮料，其供应链须满足该再生含量要求。
englishSummary: AB 793 requires plastic (PET/HDPE) beverage containers subject to California Redemption Value to meet minimum postconsumer recycled content rising from 15% (January 1, 2022) to 25% (2025) and 50% (2030), with annual reporting to CalRecycle. The obligation falls on beverage manufacturers. If Luckin sells its own bottled or plastic-cup ready-to-drink beverages in California, its supply chain must comply.
businessImpact: Luckin's own-brand bottled or sealed plastic-container RTD beverages sold in California must meet escalating recycled-content minimums, affecting supplier specs.
recommendedAction: If selling own-brand bottled/plastic-container beverages in CA, confirm suppliers meet the current PCR threshold (25% as of 2025, 50% by 2030). / 如销售自有瓶装饮料，确认供应商满足现行再生含量标准。

regulationName: SB 1046 — Solid Waste: Precheckout and Carryout Bags (Compostable/Recycled Paper Produce Bags)
chineseTitle: SB 1046 预结账/产品袋须为可堆肥或再生纸法
englishTitle: SB 1046 Precheckout Bags Must Be Compostable or Recycled Paper
agency: California Department of Resources Recycling and Recovery (CalRecycle); local enforcement
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to 'stores' providing precheckout bags in CA; narrow impact for cafés using such bags. / 适用于在加州提供预结账袋的“商店”，对使用此类袋的咖啡店影响有限。
keyRequirements: On and after January 1, 2025, a store may not provide a precheckout bag (e.g., bags for produce, pastries, or items grabbed before paying) unless it is a certified compostable bag or a recycled-paper bag meeting the statutory standard (compostable bags need a minimum 15-inch mouth width).
chineseSummary: SB 1046 自2025年1月1日起规定，商店向顾客提供的“预结账袋”（即顾客在付款前用于装散装糕点、水果等的袋子）必须是经认证的可堆肥袋或符合标准的再生纸袋，可堆肥袋开口宽度至少15英寸（便于二次用作厨余垃圾袋）。咖啡店若在自助区为顾客提供散装糕点/食品的小袋，须改用合规材质。
englishSummary: Effective January 1, 2025, SB 1046 prohibits stores from providing a precheckout bag—those used before payment, such as for loose pastries or produce—unless it is a certified compostable bag or a qualifying recycled-paper bag (compostable bags need a 15-inch minimum mouth width so they can double as kitchen food-scrap caddy liners). A café offering small bags for grab-and-go bakery items at a self-serve area must switch to compliant bags.
businessImpact: If Luckin provides small bags for loose pastries/food before checkout in California, those bags must be certified compostable or recycled paper.
recommendedAction: Swap any pre-checkout bakery/produce bags to certified-compostable or recycled-paper stock for CA stores. / 加州门店预结账糕点/食品袋改用认证可堆肥或再生纸袋。

regulationName: SB 1167 (2023–2024) — Solid Waste: Single-Use Drinking Vessels at Chain Restaurants
chineseTitle: SB 1167 连锁餐厅堂食一次性饮品杯禁令（提案）
englishTitle: SB 1167 Reusable Cups for Dine-In Chain Restaurants (Proposed)
agency: California Legislature (Sen. Catherine Blakespear); would be implemented via CalRecycle
applicabilityThreshold: Would target 'chain restaurants' (brands with more than 60 locations) for dine-in service; exact threshold subject to final bill. / 拟针对“连锁餐厅”（门店超60家品牌）堂食服务，具体门槛待最终法案确定。
keyRequirements: As introduced, SB 1167 would have prohibited a chain restaurant (defined as a brand with more than 60 locations) from serving an on-premises (dine-in) beverage in a single-use drinking vessel, instead requiring non-toxic reusable cups for customers consuming beverages on site.
chineseSummary: SB 1167 由参议员 Catherine Blakespear 于2024年2月14日提出，拟禁止连锁餐厅（定义为门店数超过60家的品牌）向堂食（店内消费）顾客以一次性杯具提供饮品，改为提供无毒可重复使用杯具，以减少加州每年约50万吨餐具废弃物。该法案在参议院环境质量委员会被搁置（held），未在2023–2024届会期推进。虽目前未成法，但反映加州对连锁餐饮一次性杯具的监管方向，咖啡连锁应予以关注。
englishSummary: Introduced February 14, 2024 by Sen. Catherine Blakespear, SB 1167 would have barred chain restaurants (defined as brands with more than 60 locations) from serving dine-in beverages in single-use drinking vessels, requiring non-toxic reusable cups for on-premises consumption to cut the ~500,000 tons of foodware waste California generates annually. The bill was held in the Senate Environmental Quality Committee and did not advance in the 2023–2024 session. It is not law, but signals a regulatory direction directly aimed at chain cafés/restaurants worth monitoring.
businessImpact: If a future version passes, Luckin would have to provide washable reusable cups (and dishwashing capacity) for all dine-in beverages in California chain locations.
recommendedAction: Monitor reintroduction of reusable-cup mandates; pilot reusable dine-in cup and warewashing workflows in CA stores. / 关注可重复杯具立法动向，在加州试点堂食可重复杯具与洗消流程。

regulationName: Commercial Food Material Disposal Ban (310 CMR 19.017)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州商业食物垃圾填埋/焚烧禁令（半吨/周门槛）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Commercial Food Material Disposal Ban
applicabilityThreshold: ≥ 1/2 ton food/organic material per week per generating site / 每个产废地点每周≥半吨食物或有机物
keyRequirements: Any business or institution generating one-half ton or more of food/organic material per week is banned from disposing, incinerating, or transferring it for disposal and must divert it via donation, composting, anaerobic digestion, or animal feed.
chineseSummary: MassDEP 的废弃物禁令规定，自2014年起对每周产生1吨及以上食物垃圾的单位实施填埋/焚烧禁令，并于2022年11月1日将门槛降至每周半吨。受规管的产废方不得将商业有机物送填埋、焚烧或转运处置，须改由捐赠、堆肥或厌氧消化等方式分流。
englishSummary: MassDEP's waste ban (310 CMR 19.017) first barred landfill/incineration disposal of food waste for generators of 1 ton or more per week (effective Oct 1, 2014), then lowered the threshold to one-half ton per week effective Nov 1, 2022. Covered generators may not dispose, incinerate, or transfer commercial organic material for disposal and must instead donate, compost, or anaerobically digest it.
businessImpact: A multi-store cafe whose combined back-of-house food scraps and coffee grounds reach a half-ton/week at a commissary or busy location must arrange organics hauling/composting or face MassDEP enforcement.
recommendedAction: 评估每个门店及任何中央厨房的每周食物垃圾量，对接持证有机物回收商；Track per-site weekly organics tonnage and contract a hauler/composter before crossing the half-ton threshold.

regulationName: MassDEP Waste Disposal Bans — Recyclable Paper, Containers & Cardboard (310 CMR 19.017)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州可回收物填埋禁令（纸张、纸板、玻璃/金属/塑料容器）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Waste Disposal Bans on Recyclables
applicabilityThreshold: All commercial waste generators statewide (no size threshold) / 全州所有商业产废方（无规模门槛）
keyRequirements: All businesses statewide are prohibited from disposing of banned recyclables — including paper, cardboard, and glass/metal/plastic containers — in trash destined for landfill or incineration, and must source-separate them for recycling.
chineseSummary: MassDEP 自1990年代起陆续颁布州废弃物禁令，禁止将纸张、纸板、玻璃/金属/塑料容器等多种可回收物作为生活垃圾填埋或焚烧（金属/玻璃容器自1992年、可回收纸张自1994年起禁运）。全州每家企业均须遵守，违者每次违规每日最高可被处以25,000美元罚款。
englishSummary: Massachusetts has banned a list of recyclable materials from landfill/incineration disposal since the 1990s under 310 CMR 19.017, including paper and cardboard (banned Dec 31, 1994) and glass/metal/plastic containers (metal/glass banned Dec 31, 1992; single-polymer plastics Dec 31, 1994). Every business in the Commonwealth must source-separate these materials for recycling, and violations can carry penalties up to $25,000 per violation per day.
businessImpact: Each cafe must provide recycling bins and keep cardboard, bottles, and cans out of the trash, or risk hauler load rejections and MassDEP penalties enforced through waste-ban inspections.
recommendedAction: 在所有门店设置容器与纸板回收流，并要求清运商提供合规分流；Set up container and cardboard recycling at every site and confirm your hauler routes them to recycling, not disposal.

regulationName: Massachusetts Uniform State Plumbing Code — Grease Interceptors (248 CMR 10.09)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州统一管道规范——隔油池/拦截器要求（248 CMR 10.09）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Plumbing Code Grease Interceptor Requirement
agency: Massachusetts Board of State Examiners of Plumbers and Gas Fitters
applicabilityThreshold: Any establishment where food is prepared producing grease-laden waste / 任何制备食物并产生含油脂废水的场所
keyRequirements: Grease interceptors/traps must be installed to receive the discharge from all fixtures and equipment that may produce grease-laden waste wherever food is prepared, sized and located per code.
chineseSummary: 马萨诸塞州统一管道规范 248 CMR 10.09 规定，凡有食物制备的场所，所有可能产生含油脂废水的器具与设备的排水都必须经过隔油池/拦截器。隔油设施的尺寸、类型与安装位置须符合规范要求。
englishSummary: Under 248 CMR 10.09 of the Massachusetts Uniform State Plumbing Code, grease interceptors/separators are required to receive discharge from all fixtures and equipment that may produce grease-laden waste wherever food is prepared. The interceptor must be of appropriate size, type, construction, and location as required by the code.
businessImpact: Any Luckin location with a kitchen sink, dishwasher, or food-prep area must have a properly sized, plumbing-inspector-approved grease interceptor before it can pass inspection and open.
recommendedAction: 在选址与装修阶段即纳入合规隔油池设计并通过管道检查；Build a code-compliant grease interceptor into store fit-out plans and clear plumbing inspection before opening.

regulationName: MWRA Sewer Use Regulations — FOG / Grease Interceptor Requirements (360 CMR 10.000)
chineseTitle: MWRA 排水使用规章——油脂(FOG)与隔油池要求（360 CMR 10.000）
englishTitle: MWRA Sewer Use Regulations (FOG)
applicabilityThreshold: Food service establishments discharging to the MWRA sewer system (Boston + member communities) / 排入MWRA下水道系统的餐饮场所（波士顿及成员社区）
