keyRequirements: Food service establishments discharging to the MWRA system must install and maintain properly sized grease traps/interceptors, may not use chemical/biological/physical means to release FOG into the sewer, and must inspect monthly and clean at the one-quarter (25%) point or at least every three months.
chineseSummary: MWRA 排水使用规章 360 CMR 10.000 禁止向其下水道系统排放违规工业废水，并要求依州法（包括 248 CMR 10.00、310 CMR 15.230）应设隔油设施的场所安装并维护合规隔油池。严禁使用化学、生物或物理手段将油脂排入下水道或绕过隔油池；须每月检查、在达到四分之一容量(25%)或至少每三个月清理一次。
englishSummary: MWRA's Sewer Use Regulations (360 CMR 10.000) govern discharges to the regional sewer system serving Greater Boston and prohibit improper FOG discharge. Establishments required by state law to have grease traps must install, maintain, and keep them effective; using chemical, biological, or physical means to release FOG into the sewer or bypass the interceptor is prohibited, and traps must be inspected monthly and cleaned at the 25% rule or at least every three months.
businessImpact: Luckin stores connected to the MWRA sewer (Boston and ~60 surrounding communities) must keep grease-trap cleaning logs and avoid enzyme/additive shortcuts, or face MWRA enforcement and back-charges.
recommendedAction: 为 MWRA 服务区门店建立隔油池每月检查与清理台账，禁用酶/化学添加剂；Keep monthly inspection and cleaning records for grease traps at MWRA-served sites and ban enzyme/chemical additives.

regulationName: Boston Water and Sewer Commission — Commercial Kitchen Grease Trap Guidelines (100 mg/L FOG limit)
chineseTitle: 波士顿水务与下水道委员会——商用厨房隔油池规定（FOG 限值100 mg/L）
englishTitle: Boston Water & Sewer Commission Grease Trap Guidelines
applicabilityThreshold: Food establishments within the City of Boston discharging FOG to the BWSC sewer / 波士顿市内向BWSC下水道排放油脂的餐饮场所
keyRequirements: Food establishments in Boston must install grease traps so that the FOG discharge concentration to the sewer does not exceed 100 mg/L, clean traps when grease reaches 25% of liquid depth (monthly minimum for point-of-use), and add no chemicals/emulsifiers/enzymes.
chineseSummary: 波士顿水务与下水道委员会规章（第三条第15节）要求，凡可能向下水道排放大量动植物油脂的场所必须安装隔油池，使排放浓度不超过100 mg/L。隔油池在油脂达到液位25%时即须清理（点位式至少每月一次、大型在地式至少每季度一次），且严禁加入漂白剂、乳化剂、酶等化学添加剂。
englishSummary: BWSC's regulations require grease traps on sewers receiving significant animal or vegetable fat, oil, or grease so that the discharge concentration does not exceed 100 milligrams per liter. Traps must be cleaned when grease reaches 25% of the liquid level (at least monthly for point-of-use traps, quarterly for large interceptors), and no bleach, emulsifiers, enzymes, or other chemicals may be added.
businessImpact: Luckin stores located in the City of Boston must meet BWSC's 100 mg/L FOG limit and maintenance schedule on top of state plumbing rules, with BWSC able to inspect, fine, and require corrective action.
recommendedAction: 波士顿门店按BWSC标准选型隔油池、保留清理记录并避免化学添加剂；For Boston sites, size grease traps to the 100 mg/L limit, retain cleaning records, and avoid all chemical additives.

regulationName: Septage Hauler Permit — Transport of Grease Trap Waste (M.G.L. c. 111 §31; 310 CMR 15.000)
chineseTitle: 化粪/隔油废物运输商许可（MGL c.111 §31；310 CMR 15.000）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Septage / Grease Waste Hauler Permit
agency: Local Boards of Health / MassDEP (Title 5, 310 CMR 15.000)
applicabilityThreshold: Any establishment generating grease-trap/septage waste requiring off-site pumping / 任何产生需外运抽吸隔油/化粪废物的场所
keyRequirements: Grease trap and septage waste may only be pumped and transported by a permitted septage hauler licensed under Title 5, and generators must use such licensed haulers for trap cleaning and off-site disposal.
chineseSummary: 依据 MGL c.111 §31 与 Title 5（310 CMR 15.000，含 15.351 与 15.500），隔油池与化粪废物只能由持证的 septage hauler 抽取和运输处置。商家在清理隔油池时必须聘用持有效许可的清运商，车辆须随车携带许可证。
englishSummary: Under M.G.L. c. 111 §31 and Title 5 (310 CMR 15.000), grease trap and septage waste must be pumped and transported only by a permitted septage hauler. Title 5 (310 CMR 15.351) requires grease traps to be inspected monthly and cleaned by a licensed septage hauler when grease reaches 25% of effective depth or at least every three months, and haulers must carry their permits in the vehicle.
businessImpact: Luckin must verify that whoever pumps its grease traps holds a valid Massachusetts septage hauler permit, since hiring an unlicensed hauler exposes the cafe to illegal-dumping liability.
recommendedAction: 签约前核验清运商的 septage hauler 许可与清运凭证留存；Verify haulers hold a valid septage permit and retain manifests/receipts for every grease-trap cleaning.

regulationName: Prohibitions on Use of Certain Hydrofluorocarbons (310 CMR 7.76)
chineseTitle: 某些氢氟碳化物(HFC)使用禁令（310 CMR 7.76）
englishTitle: Massachusetts HFC Prohibitions
applicabilityThreshold: New/retrofit commercial refrigeration & related equipment placed into service in MA after end-use phase-in date / 在用途分阶段生效日后于麻州投用的新装或改装商用制冷及相关设备
keyRequirements: It is prohibited to sell, lease, install, or manufacture new or retrofit refrigeration, chiller, aerosol, or foam equipment that uses listed high-GWP HFCs in specified end-uses after the phase-in dates (2021–2024), though existing equipment may continue operating unless substantially retrofitted.
chineseSummary: MassDEP 于2020年12月发布 310 CMR 7.76，禁止在制冷设备、冷水机组、气雾剂和泡沫等特定用途中使用某些高GWP的氢氟碳化物，禁令按用途在2021年1月1日至2024年1月1日分阶段生效（如超市制冷、独立式机组等2021年起）。现有正常运转设备无须更换，但若进行改装、扩容或累计更换超过整机重置成本50%则受限。
englishSummary: Issued in December 2020, 310 CMR 7.76 prohibits the sale, lease, installation, or manufacture of new and retrofit refrigeration, chiller, aerosol-propellant, and foam equipment using certain high-GWP HFCs in specified end-uses, phasing in between Jan 1, 2021 and Jan 1, 2024. Existing functioning equipment may keep operating and being serviced unless retrofitted, expanded, or cumulatively replaced beyond 50% of system replacement cost.
businessImpact: When Luckin buys or replaces reach-in coolers, ice machines, or other commercial refrigeration in Massachusetts, the new units must use compliant low-GWP refrigerants — a procurement and capital-planning constraint.
recommendedAction: 采购制冷设备时要求供应商确认符合 310 CMR 7.76 的低GWP制冷剂；Require refrigeration vendors to certify new units use 310 CMR 7.76-compliant low-GWP refrigerants.

regulationName: Mass Ready Act (S.3050) — Statewide Single-Use Plastic Bag Ban & Foodware-on-Request
chineseTitle: Mass Ready Act（S.3050）——全州一次性塑料袋禁令及餐具按需提供（拟议）
englishTitle: Mass Ready Act — Plastic Bag Ban & Foodware-on-Request (Proposed)
agency: Massachusetts Legislature (to be enforced by MassDEP / municipalities)
applicabilityThreshold: Statewide retail/food establishments (paper-bag fee exemption for stores with ≤10 employees at one location) / 全州零售及餐饮场所（单一地点雇员≤10人者豁免纸袋费）
keyRequirements: As passed by the Senate, the bill would ban single-use carryout plastic bags statewide, require a minimum 10-cent fee for recycled paper bags, and require single-use foodware items like straws, stirrers, and utensils to be provided only on customer request. The House version (June 2026) dropped these single-use plastics provisions.
chineseSummary: Mass Ready Act（S.3050，全称『An Act to build resilience for Massachusetts communities』，源自 Healey 州长2025年提案）是一项约36.4亿美元的环境韧性债券法案。参议院于2026年4月15日通过的版本含全州一次性塑料购物袋禁令、纸袋至少收取10美分费用以及吸管、搅拌棒、餐具等一次性餐具按需提供条款。但据《波士顿环球报》2026年6月16日报道，众议院拨款委员会版本删除了上述一次性塑料相关条款；该法案尚未成为法律，仍待众议院表决及两院协调。
englishSummary: The Mass Ready Act (S.3050, 'An Act to build resilience for Massachusetts communities'), a ~$3.64 billion environmental bond bill stemming from Gov. Healey's 2025 proposal, was passed by the Senate on April 15, 2026 with a statewide ban on single-use carryout plastic bags, a minimum 10-cent recycled-paper-bag fee, and a foodware-on-request requirement for items such as straws, stirrers, and utensils. However, per the Boston Globe (June 16, 2026), the House Ways & Means version DROPPED these single-use-plastics provisions. The bill is not yet law and any plastics provisions would have to be restored in House debate or conference committee.
businessImpact: If the plastics provisions are restored and enacted, Luckin would have to stop offering plastic carryout bags statewide, charge for paper bags, and hold back straws/stirrers/utensils unless customers ask — affecting packaging procurement and front-counter workflow. As of June 2026 the House version omitted these provisions, so the outcome is uncertain.
recommendedAction: 跟踪 S.3050 在众议院及两院协调委员会中一次性塑料条款的去留，预先评估纸袋收费与按需餐具的POS与培训改造；Monitor whether S.3050's single-use-plastics provisions survive House debate/conference and pre-plan paper-bag fee POS changes and request-only foodware SOPs.

regulationName: Better Bottle Bill Expansion — Modernization of the Beverage Container Deposit (Proposed Amendment to M.G.L. c. 94 §321 et seq.)
chineseTitle: 升级版瓶单法案——饮料容器押金现代化（拟议修订）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Better Bottle Bill Expansion (Proposed)
applicabilityThreshold: Retailers/dealers selling deposit-eligible beverage containers statewide / 全州销售可押金饮料容器的零售商
keyRequirements: If enacted, the expansion would add noncarbonated beverages, water, wine, spirits, and 'nip' bottles to the deposit system and raise the container deposit from 5 cents to 10 cents.
chineseSummary: 麻州参议院曾于2024年6月以修正案形式通过升级瓶单法案，拟将非碳酸饮料、水、葡萄酒、烈酒及小酒瓶('nips')纳入押金体系，并将押金从5美分提高到10美分。据预测每年可多回收约31亿个容器，但该扩展多次在众议院搁置，目前仍属拟议状态、尚未成为法律。
englishSummary: The 'Better Bottle Bill' would modernize the Massachusetts deposit system by adding noncarbonated drinks, water, wine, spirits, and 'nip' bottles to deposit-eligible containers and raising the deposit from 5 to 10 cents. The Senate passed such an amendment in June 2024 (projected to recycle ~3.1 billion more containers annually), but the expansion has repeatedly stalled in the House and remains proposed, not enacted.
businessImpact: If expanded, more of the bottled beverages Luckin sells (water, juices, RTD drinks) would carry a 10-cent deposit, requiring redemption handling, label checks, and possibly in-store take-back.
recommendedAction: 跟踪瓶单扩展立法，预估对瓶装饮料采购与回收处理的影响；Track bottle-bill expansion bills and assess impact on bottled-beverage SKUs and deposit handling.

regulationName: Massachusetts NPDES Stormwater Permitting & Stormwater Management Standards
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州雨水排放许可与雨水管理标准（NPDES / 雨水手册）
englishTitle: Massachusetts NPDES Stormwater Permitting & Standards
agency: U.S. EPA Region 1 & Massachusetts Department of Environmental Protection (MassDEP)
applicabilityThreshold: New development/redevelopment or weather-exposed industrial activity sites / 新建或改建工程及暴露于天气的工业活动场地
keyRequirements: Sites with industrial activity exposed to weather may need coverage under the EPA Multi-Sector General Permit, and new development/redevelopment must meet MassDEP Stormwater Management Standards controlling runoff quantity and quality (e.g., best management practices, no untreated discharges).
chineseSummary: 麻州雨水管理由 EPA 一区与 MassDEP 共同监管：暴露于天气的工业活动场地可能需纳入 EPA 多行业通用许可(MSGP)，新建/改建工程须符合 MassDEP《雨水管理标准》，对径流水量水质加以控制。一般咖啡门店通常不属于工业类别，但若涉及车辆维护、户外物料堆放或新建工程则需关注合规。
englishSummary: Stormwater discharges in Massachusetts are regulated jointly by EPA Region 1 and MassDEP. Facilities with weather-exposed industrial activity may require coverage under the EPA Multi-Sector General Permit, and new development/redevelopment must satisfy the MassDEP Stormwater Management Standards governing runoff quantity and quality. A typical limited-service cafe is generally not an MSGP industrial sector, but site construction, drive-thru paving, or outdoor storage can trigger stormwater obligations.
businessImpact: When Luckin builds out a new freestanding store or drive-thru, the construction and site design must meet stormwater standards (and possibly a construction general permit), affecting site plans and permitting timelines.
recommendedAction: 新建门店时由土建顾问核查是否需要施工期雨水许可及雨水管理标准合规；For ground-up/drive-thru builds, have site engineers confirm construction stormwater permit and Stormwater Standards compliance.

regulationName: Solid Waste Hauler Waste-Ban Compliance Obligations (310 CMR 19.017)
chineseTitle: 固体废物清运商废弃物禁令合规义务（310 CMR 19.017）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Solid Waste Hauler Waste-Ban Compliance
applicabilityThreshold: All commercial generators and their solid-waste haulers statewide / 全州所有商业产废方及其固废清运商
keyRequirements: Both generators and the haulers that transport their waste are jointly responsible for keeping banned materials (recyclables and, above threshold, organics) out of disposal-bound loads, and haulers may reject or surcharge contaminated loads.
chineseSummary: 在 310 CMR 19.017 下，产废方与运输其废物的清运商都对遵守废弃物禁令负有责任——禁运物（可回收物以及达门槛的有机物）不得混入填埋/焚烧载运中。清运商可拒收或对含禁运物的载运加收费用，产废方因此须选择能配合分流的合规清运商。
englishSummary: Under 310 CMR 19.017, both waste generators and the haulers transporting their waste share responsibility for keeping banned materials out of disposal-bound loads. Haulers are subject to waste-ban compliance and may reject or surcharge contaminated loads, so generators must use haulers that support required recycling and (above threshold) organics diversion.
businessImpact: Luckin's choice of trash hauler matters: contracting with a hauler that won't separate recyclables/organics can lead to rejected loads, surcharges, and shared waste-ban liability.
recommendedAction: 选择能提供回收（及达标时有机物）分流服务的合规清运商并写入合同；Contract only with haulers that provide compliant recycling and (above threshold) organics diversion, and document it.

regulationName: DC Water Fats, Oils & Grease (FOG) Control Program for Food Service Establishments
chineseTitle: 华盛顿特区供水局餐饮场所油脂(FOG)管控计划
englishTitle: DC Water FOG Control Program for Food Service Establishments
agency: DC Water (District of Columbia Water and Sewer Authority)
applicabilityThreshold: Any food service establishment preparing/serving food in the District / 在特区内准备或供应食物的任何餐饮场所
keyRequirements: Every food service establishment must register with DC Water, install and maintain a properly sized grease interceptor per the DC Plumbing Code, keep FOG in wastewater within discharge limits, and clean interceptors on a set schedule (gravity every 90 days or at 25% capacity; hydromechanical/grease removal devices every 30 days or at 50%) with records retained.
chineseSummary: 所有在特区准备或供应食物的商业场所都必须在DC Water的FOG最佳管理实践门户登记，并按特区管道规范安装与维护合规油脂截留器。重力式截留器须每90天或装满25%时清理，机械式与油脂去除装置须每30天或装满50%时清理，并保留清理记录以备DC Water检查。
englishSummary: All commercial food-preparing establishments in the District must register with DC Water's FOG BMP portal and install and maintain a compliant grease interceptor under the DC Plumbing Code. Gravity interceptors must be cleaned every 90 days or at 25% capacity, while hydromechanical units and grease removal devices must be cleaned every 30 days or at 50%, with cleaning records kept for DC Water inspection.
businessImpact: A Luckin café in DC must register with DC Water, fit and maintain a grease interceptor, and log pump-outs, or face violations, fees, and potential enforcement.
recommendedAction: Register the store with DC Water's FOG portal before opening; size and install a code-compliant grease interceptor and set a pump-out + record-keeping schedule. 开业前在DC Water登记并安装合规油脂截留器，建立清理与记录制度。

regulationName: Compostable or Recyclable Disposable Food Service Ware Required (Sustainable DC Omnibus Amendment Act of 2014; D.C. Code § 8-1533) — incl. Expanded Polystyrene Foam Ban (§ 8-1532)
chineseTitle: 一次性餐具须可堆肥或可回收规定(含发泡聚苯乙烯禁令)
englishTitle: Compostable/Recyclable Food Service Ware Required & Foam Ban (D.C. Code § 8-1533, § 8-1532)
agency: District Department of Energy and Environment (DOEE)
applicabilityThreshold: Any business selling or serving food/beverages in the District / 在特区销售或供应食物饮料的任何商家
keyRequirements: Businesses that sell or serve food/beverages in the District must use only food service ware (containers, cups, plates, cutlery) determined by DOEE to be recyclable or compostable (D.C. Code § 8-1533), and may not use expanded polystyrene (foam) food service products (D.C. Code § 8-1532).
chineseSummary: 依据《可持续特区综合修正法案(2014)》，自2016年1月1日起禁止使用发泡聚苯乙烯(泡沫)餐具(D.C. Code §8-1532)，自2017年1月1日起所有在特区供应食物或饮料的商家只能使用经DOEE认定为可回收或可堆肥的一次性餐具(D.C. Code §8-1533)。DOEE维护合规与禁用材料清单并负责执法。
englishSummary: Under the Sustainable DC Omnibus Amendment Act of 2014, foam (expanded polystyrene) food service products were banned from January 1, 2016 (D.C. Code § 8-1532), and from January 1, 2017 all District food/beverage-serving businesses must use only disposable food service ware that DOEE has determined to be recyclable or compostable (D.C. Code § 8-1533). DOEE maintains the approved and prohibited materials lists and enforces the rule.
businessImpact: Every cup, lid, straw, stirrer, and takeout container Luckin uses in DC must appear on DOEE's compostable/recyclable list and contain no banned foam, constraining packaging sourcing.
recommendedAction: Audit all DC store disposables against DOEE's approved/prohibited list and swap out any foam or non-listed items before opening. 开业前对照DOEE清单核查所有一次性用品，淘汰泡沫及未列名材料。

regulationName: Disposable Food Service Accessories By Request Only (Zero Waste Omnibus Amendment Act of 2020; D.C. Law 23-211)
chineseTitle: 一次性餐具配件仅按需提供规定(2020零废物综合修正法案)
englishTitle: Accessory Disposable Foodware By Request Only (D.C. Law 23-211)
agency: District Department of Energy and Environment (DOEE)
applicabilityThreshold: All District restaurants and food-serving entities, plus third-party delivery platforms / 特区所有餐厅、供餐机构及第三方外卖平台
keyRequirements: Food-serving entities may not automatically include accessory disposable foodware (utensils, chopsticks, condiment packets, napkins, straws/stirrers) in orders and must provide them only when affirmatively requested by the customer or via self-serve; third-party platforms must let customers individually opt in (effective July 1, 2021 for platforms).
chineseSummary: 依据《零废物综合修正法案(2020)》(D.C. Law 23-211)，自2022年1月1日起，特区餐饮商家不得在订单中自动附带一次性餐具配件(餐具、筷子、调料包、餐巾、吸管/搅拌棒)，只能在顾客主动索取或自取区提供。自2021年7月1日起，第三方点餐平台须让顾客逐项勾选所需配件，由DOEE执法。
englishSummary: Under the Zero Waste Omnibus Amendment Act of 2020 (D.C. Law 23-211), effective January 1, 2022, District food-serving entities are prohibited from automatically including accessory disposables (utensils, chopsticks, condiments, napkins, straws/stirrers) in orders and must provide them only on request or via self-service. Since July 1, 2021, third-party ordering platforms must let customers select individual items rather than a single all-disposables checkbox. DOEE enforces.
businessImpact: Luckin must reconfigure DC in-store and delivery/app workflows so straws, utensils, and napkins are by-request-only rather than auto-added.
recommendedAction: Move accessory disposables behind the counter / to self-serve and configure delivery apps for item-by-item opt-in for DC stores. 将一次性配件改为柜台索取或自取，并在外卖平台设置逐项选择。

regulationName: Requirements for Commercial Food Waste — Source Separation (Zero Waste Omnibus Amendment Act of 2020; D.C. Code § 8-1031.03a)
chineseTitle: 商业食物垃圾源头分类要求(D.C. Code §8-1031.03a)
englishTitle: Commercial Food Waste Source Separation (D.C. Code § 8-1031.03a)
agency: District Department of Energy and Environment (DOEE) / Department of Public Works
applicabilityThreshold: Retail food stores ≥10,000 sq ft and other listed large generators / ≥10,000平方英尺零售食品店及其他列明大型产生者
keyRequirements: Covered large food-waste generators must source-separate all back-of-house commercial food waste and arrange separate collection to an organic-waste processing facility or compost on-site; thresholds include retail food stores ≥10,000 sq ft (effective 2023) and store chains (3+ stores, 10,000+ combined sq ft), stadiums (15,000+ seats), and hospitals/nursing homes (300+ beds) (effective 2024).
chineseSummary: 依据D.C. Code §8-1031.03a，特定大型食物垃圾产生者须将后厨商业食物垃圾源头分类，单独运往有机废物处理设施或就地堆肥。门槛包括≥10,000平方英尺的零售食品店(2023年生效)，以及连锁店(3家以上、合计10,000平方英尺以上)、可容纳15,000人以上的场馆、300床以上的医院/疗养院(2024年生效)。一般独立咖啡店未达门槛，但反映特区有机物分流方向。
englishSummary: Under D.C. Code § 8-1031.03a (Zero Waste Omnibus Amendment Act of 2020), covered large food-waste generators must source-separate all back-of-house food waste and send it to an organics processor or compost on-site. Thresholds include retail food stores of at least 10,000 sq ft from January 1, 2023, and store chains (3+ stores, 10,000+ combined sq ft), large stadiums, and hospitals/nursing homes from January 1, 2024. A standalone café is below the size thresholds but the rule signals the District's organics-diversion trajectory.
businessImpact: A typical Luckin café sits below the 10,000 sq ft trigger, so this is currently informational, but a large flagship or commissary could be pulled in and future expansions of the threshold are likely.
recommendedAction: Confirm DC store square footage stays under 10,000 sq ft; monitor DOEE threshold expansions and pilot back-of-house organics separation voluntarily. 核实特区门店面积低于10,000平方英尺，关注DOEE门槛扩展并自愿试行后厨分类。

regulationName: Solid Waste Collector Registration and Reporting (D.C. Code §§ 8-1031.05 and 8-1031.06)
chineseTitle: 固体废物收运商登记与报告制度(D.C. Code §§8-1031.05、8-1031.06)
englishTitle: Solid Waste Collector Registration & Reporting (D.C. Code §§ 8-1031.05/.06)
agency: Department of Public Works — Office of Waste Diversion (OWD)
applicabilityThreshold: Entities that collect/transport solid waste in the District (typically the hauler, not the café) / 在特区收运固体废物的主体(通常为收运商而非咖啡店)
keyRequirements: Any person or company that collects or transports solid waste (including recycling/organics) in the District must register annually (by February 1) with DPW's Office of Waste Diversion and report prior-year tonnages, paying $50 per collector plus $50 per collection vehicle, in addition to any business license.
chineseSummary: 依据D.C. Code §§8-1031.05与8-1031.06，凡在特区收集或运输固体废物(含可回收物与有机物)的个人或公司，须每年(2月1日前)向DPW废物分流办公室登记并报告上一年度吨数，每个收运商缴费50美元、每辆收运车辆50美元，且此登记是在营业执照之外的额外要求。
englishSummary: Under D.C. Code §§ 8-1031.05 and 8-1031.06, any person or company collecting or transporting solid waste (including recyclables and organics) in the District must register annually with DPW's Office of Waste Diversion (by February 1 each year) and report prior-year tonnages. Fees are $50 per collector plus $50 per collection vehicle, in addition to any business license.
businessImpact: Luckin itself contracts a hauler rather than hauling, so this mainly governs Luckin's waste vendor — but Luckin should verify its DC hauler is properly registered to avoid chain-of-custody compliance gaps.
recommendedAction: Require proof of current DPW Office of Waste Diversion registration from any DC waste/recycling hauler in vendor contracts. 在供应商合同中要求特区废物/回收收运商提供DPW废物分流办公室有效登记证明。

regulationName: Recycling Refund and Litter Reduction Amendment Act of 2025 (DC Bottle Bill, B26-0058)
chineseTitle: 2025回收返还与减少垃圾修正法案(特区瓶子法案,B26-0058)
englishTitle: Recycling Refund and Litter Reduction Amendment Act of 2025 (B26-0058)
agency: Council of the District of Columbia / District Department of Energy and Environment (DOEE)
applicabilityThreshold: Beverage distributors and retailers selling covered containers in the District / 在特区销售受涵盖容器的饮料经销商与零售商
keyRequirements: Would establish an extended-producer-responsibility deposit program (implemented by Jan 1, 2028) requiring distributors to place a refundable deposit of at least 10 cents (potentially rising to 15 cents if redemption targets are missed) on beverage containers (plastic bottles, cans, glass), refunded on return for recycling, run by a distributor-funded nonprofit and overseen/enforced by DOEE.
chineseSummary: B26-0058号《回收返还与减少垃圾修正法案》于2025年1月由议会主席Mendelson及Nadeau等多名议员提出，拟建立延伸生产者责任押金制度(2028年1月1日前实施)，要求经销商对饮料容器(塑料瓶、易拉罐、玻璃瓶)征收至少10美分可退押金(若回收率未达标可升至15美分)，消费者退还容器回收时可获退款。计划由饮料经销商出资的非营利机构运营，DOEE监督执法。2025年10月1日已举行公开听证，仍处于委员会审议阶段。
englishSummary: B26-0058, the Recycling Refund and Litter Reduction Amendment Act of 2025, was introduced January 2025 by Council Chairman Phil Mendelson with Councilmember Brianne Nadeau and colleagues. It would establish an extended-producer-responsibility deposit program (implemented by Jan 1, 2028) with a refundable deposit of at least 10 cents (rising to 15 cents if redemption targets are missed) on beverage containers (plastic bottles, cans, glass), refunded when returned for recycling, run by a distributor-funded nonprofit and overseen by DOEE. A public hearing was held October 1, 2025; the bill remains in committee.
businessImpact: If enacted, Luckin's DC stores selling bottled/canned beverages would need to add deposits at point of sale and potentially handle container redemption, affecting pricing and operations.
recommendedAction: Track B26-0058 through DC Council; model POS and pricing impact for bottled/canned beverages sold in DC stores. 跟踪B26-0058立法进程，测算特区门店瓶装/罐装饮料的收银与定价影响。

regulationName: Stormwater Management Performance Requirements (Title 21 DCMR, Chapter 5; § 21-520)
chineseTitle: 雨水管理性能要求(21 DCMR第5章 §21-520)
englishTitle: Stormwater Management Performance Requirements (21 DCMR § 21-520)
agency: District Department of Energy and Environment (DOEE)
applicabilityThreshold: Major land-disturbing activity / substantial improvement (size-gated construction) / 重大土地扰动或重大改建(按规模触发)
keyRequirements: Major land-disturbing or substantial-improvement projects must retain on-site the Stormwater Retention Volume from the 90th-percentile (1.2-inch) storm (80th-percentile / 0.8-inch for substantial improvement), achieving at least 50% on-site, typically via green infrastructure, with the remainder met through retention credits or in-lieu fee.
chineseSummary: 依据21 DCMR第5章(§21-520等)，特区重大土地扰动或重大改建项目须就地滞留相当于第90百分位(1.2英寸)降雨事件的雨水滞留量(重大改建为第80百分位/0.8英寸)，其中至少50%须就地实现，通常采用绿色基础设施，其余可通过雨水滞留信用或代缴费满足。由DOEE管理。
englishSummary: Under Title 21 DCMR Chapter 5 (§ 21-520 and related), major land-disturbing activity and major substantial-improvement projects must retain on-site the Stormwater Retention Volume from the 90th-percentile (1.2-inch) storm (80th-percentile / 0.8-inch for substantial improvement), with at least 50% achieved on-site, typically through green infrastructure, and the balance met via Stormwater Retention Credits or an in-lieu fee. DOEE administers the standards.
businessImpact: Relevant when Luckin does a major build-out or substantial renovation of a DC location — triggering stormwater retention design obligations usually shared with the landlord/developer.
recommendedAction: Flag stormwater retention obligations in lease/build-out agreements for any major DC construction or substantial improvement. 在特区重大施工或改建的租约/装修协议中标注雨水滞留义务。

regulationName: Septage and Food Establishment Sludge — Grease Interceptor Pump-Out Permit (Fla. Admin. Code R. 62-6.010)
chineseTitle: 化粪与餐饮场所污泥——油脂截留器抽吸许可(F.A.C. 62-6.010)
englishTitle: Septage & Food Establishment Sludge / Grease Interceptor Pump-Out Permit (F.A.C. 62-6.010)
applicabilityThreshold: Food establishments with grease interceptors on onsite/septic systems; statewide hauler permitting / 现场/化粪系统上装有油脂截留器的餐饮场所；全州收运商许可
keyRequirements: No grease interceptor or septic tank associated with an onsite sewage treatment and disposal system may be cleaned or pumped out until the servicing company holds a current annual DEP operating permit, and food-establishment sludge/grease must be hauled and disposed of by a permitted service with proper records.
chineseSummary: 依据《佛州行政法典》62-6.010(原卫生部64E-6，2021年7月1日依《清洁水道法案》转由DEP管辖)，与现场污水处理系统相关的油脂截留器或化粪池，须由持有DEP有效年度运营许可的服务公司方可清理或抽吸；餐饮场所油脂污泥须由持证服务商按规运输处置并保存记录。佛州无单一州级油脂法，截留器安装另依《佛州建筑规范-管道卷》及地方FOG计划。
englishSummary: Under Fla. Admin. Code R. 62-6.010 (formerly DOH Chapter 64E-6, transferred to DEP effective July 1, 2021 under the Clean Waterways Act), no grease interceptor or septic tank tied to an onsite sewage treatment and disposal system may be cleaned or pumped out until the servicing company holds a current annual DEP operating permit. Food-establishment grease/sludge must be hauled and disposed of by a permitted service with proper records. Interceptor installation itself is governed by the Florida Building Code Plumbing volume and local FOG programs.
businessImpact: A Luckin café on a septic/onsite system in Florida must use a DEP-permitted hauler to pump its grease interceptor and retain manifests, and check the local utility's FOG program for sizing/cleaning rules.
recommendedAction: Use only DEP-permitted grease/septage haulers in FL, retain pump-out manifests, and confirm local utility FOG requirements per site. 佛州门店仅使用DEP持证油脂收运商，保存抽吸记录，并逐店核实当地FOG要求。

regulationName: State Preemption of Polystyrene Regulation (Fla. Stat. § 500.90)
chineseTitle: 聚苯乙烯监管州级先占(佛州法规 §500.90)
englishTitle: State Preemption of Polystyrene Regulation (Fla. Stat. § 500.90)
agency: Florida Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services (entities regulated under Chapter 500)
applicabilityThreshold: Food establishments regulated under Fla. Stat. Chapter 500 (statewide) / 受佛州法规第500章管辖的食品企业(全州)
keyRequirements: Regulation of the use or sale of polystyrene (foam) products by entities regulated under Chapter 500 (food establishments) is preempted to the state, so local Florida governments generally may not ban or restrict foam foodware by food businesses, subject to limited exceptions (pre-2016 ordinances, public property, public contracts).
chineseSummary: 佛州法规§500.90规定，受第500章管辖的食品企业对聚苯乙烯(泡沫)产品的使用或销售之监管权先占归州所有，因此佛州地方政府一般不得禁止或限制食品企业使用泡沫餐具。例外包括2016年1月1日前已通过的地方条例、对公共财产上个人/临时摊贩的限制以及政府合同关系。对营运者而言这是利好的先占条款。
englishSummary: Fla. Stat. § 500.90 preempts to the state the regulation of the use or sale of polystyrene (foam) products by entities regulated under Chapter 500 (food establishments), effective January 1, 2016. Florida local governments therefore generally cannot ban or restrict foam foodware used by food businesses, with limited exceptions for pre-2016 ordinances, use on public property, and government contracts.
businessImpact: This preemption protects Luckin in Florida — local foam bans cannot be enforced against its packaging — but it is informational rather than a compliance obligation, and could shift if the Legislature changes the law.
recommendedAction: Treat FL foam packaging as permitted statewide but monitor for any repeal of § 500.90 and check pre-2016 local ordinances. 视佛州泡沫包装为全州可用，但关注§500.90是否被废止并核查2016年前地方条例。

regulationName: Florida 75% Recycling Goal and Recycling Reporting (Fla. Stat. § 403.7032)
chineseTitle: 佛州75%回收目标与回收报告(佛州法规 §403.7032)
englishTitle: Florida 75% Recycling Goal & Reporting (Fla. Stat. § 403.7032)
applicabilityThreshold: Statewide 75% goal; county program obligations and the 75,000-or-fewer 'opportunity to recycle' option under § 403.706; business reporting encouraged / 全州75%目标;县级计划义务及人口75,000及以下的「回收机会」选项见§403.706;鼓励企业报告
keyRequirements: Sets a statewide long-term goal to recycle at least 75% of municipal solid waste by 2020, directs DEP to run a recycling program, and encourages (does not require) private businesses to report recycled tonnages to the county annually. County recycling-program obligations and the 'opportunity to recycle' option for counties of 75,000 or fewer residents are set under the related Fla. Stat. § 403.706.
chineseSummary: 佛州法规§403.7032由2008年HB 7135设立，确立至2020年州内回收至少75%城市固体废物的长期目标，授权DEP制定回收计划。私营企业被鼓励(非强制)每年向所在县报告其回收的纸、金属、玻璃、塑料等吨数。县级回收计划义务及人口75,000及以下的县可选用「提供回收机会」替代目标，规定于相关条文§403.706。这影响咖啡店所在县的商业回收基础设施与服务安排。
englishSummary: Fla. Stat. § 403.7032, created by HB 7135 in 2008, establishes a statewide long-term goal to recycle at least 75% of municipal solid waste by 2020 and directs DEP to administer a recycling program. Private businesses are encouraged (not mandated) to report recycled tonnages of paper, metal, glass, plastics, etc. annually to the county. County recycling-program obligations — including that counties of 75,000 or fewer residents may instead provide an 'opportunity to recycle' — are set under the related Fla. Stat. § 403.706.
businessImpact: Drives the commercial-recycling infrastructure and county programs that determine how (and whether) Luckin's Florida stores can recycle cups, cardboard, and other materials.
recommendedAction: Enroll FL stores in county commercial recycling programs and track cardboard/cup recycling for ESG reporting. 让佛州门店加入所在县商业回收计划，记录纸板/杯子回收量用于ESG报告。

regulationName: Florida Building Code, Energy Conservation — Commercial Water-Efficiency / Plumbing Fixture Requirements
chineseTitle: 佛州建筑规范·节能卷——商业节水/管道器具要求
englishTitle: Florida Building Code, Energy Conservation — Commercial Water Efficiency / Plumbing Fixtures
agency: Florida Building Commission / local building officials (Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation)
applicabilityThreshold: New construction and substantial renovation of commercial buildings in Florida / 佛州商业建筑的新建与重大改建
keyRequirements: Commercial buildings must meet water-efficiency provisions of the Florida Building Code (lavatory/sink flow limited to 2.5 gpm at 80 psi and other fixture maximums) and energy provisions for service water heating, including accessible controls and demand-based circulating-pump controls.
chineseSummary: 佛州建筑规范·节能卷(第8版,2023年版,2023年12月31日生效)对商业建筑规定节水与服务热水要求：水龙头/洗涤池流量在80 psi下不超过2.5加仑/分钟等器具上限，并要求热水循环系统设可达控制装置与按需启泵控制。Luckin在佛州新建或大改门店时，管道器具与热水系统须符合此规范方能通过验收。
englishSummary: The Florida Building Code, Energy Conservation (8th Edition, 2023, effective December 31, 2023) sets water-efficiency and service-water-heating requirements for commercial buildings, including lavatory/sink flow limited to 2.5 gpm at 80 psi and other fixture maximums, plus accessible and demand-based controls for hot-water circulating systems. When Luckin builds out or substantially renovates a Florida store, its plumbing fixtures and water-heating systems must comply to pass inspection.
businessImpact: Constrains fixture and water-heater specs for Luckin's Florida store build-outs; non-compliant fixtures will fail building inspection and delay opening.
recommendedAction: Specify code-compliant low-flow fixtures and water-heater controls in FL build-out drawings and confirm at permit/inspection. 在佛州装修图纸中指定合规低流量器具与热水控制，并在报建/验收时确认。

regulationName: Clean Water Act Section 402 — National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES) Permit Program
chineseTitle: 《清洁水法》第402条 — 国家污染物排放消除制度(NPDES)许可计划
englishTitle: Clean Water Act §402 NPDES Permit Program
agency: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) / authorized state agencies
applicabilityThreshold: Any facility discharging pollutants from a point source to waters of the U.S. (cafés typically exempt via sewer/POTW). / 从点源向美国水域排放污染物的设施(咖啡店通常经下水道豁免)。
keyRequirements: No pollutant may be discharged from any point source to waters of the United States without an NPDES permit specifying effluent limitations.
chineseSummary: 1972年《清洁水法》第402条建立了NPDES许可制度,禁止在未获许可的情况下从任何"点源"(管道、沟渠等)向美国水域排放污染物。绝大多数咖啡店通过下水道排入公共污水处理厂(POTW),由地方预处理计划而非直接NPDES许可管辖,但该联邦框架是所有州及地方废水、暴雨水排放规则的法律基础。
englishSummary: Section 402 of the 1972 Clean Water Act created the NPDES program, prohibiting discharge of pollutants from any point source to waters of the U.S. without a permit setting effluent limits. Most cafés discharge to a POTW via the sewer and are governed by local pretreatment programs rather than a direct NPDES permit, but this federal framework is the legal foundation for every state and local wastewater and stormwater discharge rule.
businessImpact: It is the foundational federal authority under which a café's sewer-discharge, grease, and stormwater obligations are ultimately derived and enforced.
recommendedAction: Confirm each site discharges to a permitted POTW and comply with local pretreatment/grease rules; direct NPDES permits are normally not needed for a café. / 确认各门店排入持证POTW并遵守地方预处理规定。

regulationName: AB 341 — Mandatory Commercial Recycling
chineseTitle: AB 341 — 强制性商业回收法
englishTitle: California Mandatory Commercial Recycling (AB 341)
agency: California Department of Resources Recycling and Recovery (CalRecycle)
applicabilityThreshold: Business generating ≥4 cubic yards of solid waste per week. / 每周产生≥4立方码固体废物的企业。
keyRequirements: A business generating four or more cubic yards of commercial solid waste per week must arrange for recycling services (self-haul, subscribe to a service, or donate/sell recyclables).
chineseSummary: 加州AB 341自2012年7月1日起生效,要求每周产生4立方码或以上商业固体废物的企业,以及5个或以上单元的多户住宅,安排回收服务。该法由CalRecycle执行,旨在推动全州75%的废物分流目标。
englishSummary: California's AB 341, effective July 1, 2012, requires any business generating four or more cubic yards of commercial solid waste per week (and multifamily dwellings of five-plus units) to arrange for recycling services. Enforced by CalRecycle, it supports the state's 75% waste-diversion goal and is administered locally through jurisdictions' franchised haulers.
businessImpact: Any California café meeting the 4-cubic-yard threshold must subscribe to recycling collection and properly separate recyclables or face local enforcement.
recommendedAction: Subscribe to recycling service at each CA location generating ≥4 cu yd/week and train staff on separation. / 在加州各达标门店订购回收服务并培训员工分类。

regulationName: CalGreen (Title 24, Part 11) §5.303 — Nonresidential Indoor Water Use Reduction
chineseTitle: CalGreen(第24编第11部)§5.303 — 非住宅室内用水削减
englishTitle: CalGreen Nonresidential Water Use Reduction (§5.303)
agency: California Building Standards Commission / local building departments
applicabilityThreshold: New construction and substantial renovations/tenant improvements of nonresidential buildings in CA. / 加州非住宅建筑的新建及重大改建/租户装修。
keyRequirements: New and substantially renovated nonresidential buildings must achieve at least a 20% reduction in indoor potable water use through low-flow plumbing fixtures and fittings.
chineseSummary: 加州绿色建筑标准规范(CalGreen,第24编第11部)第5.303节为强制性措施,要求新建及大型改建的非住宅建筑(含餐饮设施)室内饮用水使用量比基准至少减少20%,通过低流量马桶、龙头等达成。由地方建筑部门在施工许可阶段执行,对新建或翻新门店具约束力。
englishSummary: CalGreen (California Green Building Standards Code, Title 24 Part 11) Section 5.303 is a mandatory measure requiring new and substantially renovated nonresidential buildings to cut indoor potable water use by at least 20% below baseline using low-flow water closets, faucets, and fittings. It is enforced by local building departments at permit/plan-check stage and binds any new or remodeled café build-out in California.
businessImpact: Every new or remodeled California café build-out must install fixtures meeting the 20% water-reduction standard to pass plan check and obtain a building permit.
recommendedAction: Specify CalGreen-compliant low-flow fixtures in all CA store designs and document the 20% reduction for plan check. / 在加州门店设计中采用符合CalGreen的低流量洁具并提供削减证明。

regulationName: Expanded Polystyrene Foam Container & Polystyrene Loose Fill Packaging Ban (ECL Article 27, Title 30)
chineseTitle: 发泡聚苯乙烯泡沫容器及散装填充包装禁令(ECL第27条第30章)
englishTitle: NYS Polystyrene Foam Container Ban
agency: New York State Department of Environmental Conservation (DEC)
applicabilityThreshold: All food service providers and stores statewide (no size threshold). / 全州所有餐饮服务者及商店(无规模门槛)。
keyRequirements: No covered food service provider, store, or manufacturer may sell, offer for sale, or distribute disposable expanded-polystyrene foam food service containers or polystyrene loose fill packaging in New York State.
chineseSummary: 纽约州自2022年1月1日起在全州范围内禁止餐饮服务提供者、商店及制造商销售或分发一次性发泡聚苯乙烯(EPS)泡沫餐饮容器及聚苯乙烯散装填充包装(花生泡沫)。违规罚款自250美元起递增,由州环保署(DEC)执行;该州级禁令独立于纽约市的EPS禁令。
englishSummary: Effective January 1, 2022, New York State prohibits any covered food service provider, store, or manufacturer from selling or distributing disposable expanded-polystyrene (EPS) foam food-service containers and polystyrene loose-fill packing peanuts statewide. Fines start at $250 and escalate; the DEC enforces it under ECL Article 27, Title 30. This statewide ban operates independently of, and in addition to, NYC's own EPS ban.
businessImpact: Cafés operating anywhere in New York State must source non-foam cups, lids, and clamshells, not just within New York City.
recommendedAction: Verify all foodware across NYS sites is foam-free; audit suppliers for EPS items. / 核查纽约州所有门店餐具均无泡沫并审查供应商。

regulationName: Air Pollution Control Permits and Registrations (6 NYCRR Part 201)
chineseTitle: 空气污染控制许可与登记(6 NYCRR 第201部)
englishTitle: NYS Air Permits & Registrations (6 NYCRR Part 201)
agency: New York State Department of Environmental Conservation (DEC)
applicabilityThreshold: Stationary sources of air emissions; threshold determines registration vs. permit. / 固定空气排放源,门槛决定登记或许可。
keyRequirements: Stationary air emission sources must obtain the appropriate authorization — a Title V permit (major sources), State Facility permit (minor sources), or air facility registration (smaller/exempt sources) — based on emission thresholds.
chineseSummary: 纽约州环保署(DEC)依据6 NYCRR第201部对固定空气排放源实施许可与登记制度:大型源需Title V许可,非大型源需州设施许可,较小源需空气设施登记。典型咖啡店烘焙/烹饪设备的排放量通常低于门槛,可能仅需登记或属豁免,但全州扩张时(尤其含店内烘焙)须逐址核实。
englishSummary: Under 6 NYCRR Part 201, the NYS DEC requires stationary air-emission sources to hold the correct authorization — a Title V permit (major sources), a State Facility permit (minor sources), or an air facility registration (smaller/exempt sources) — keyed to emission thresholds. A typical café's cooking or roasting equipment usually falls below permit thresholds and may only need registration or qualify as exempt, but on-site roasting operations should be checked site-by-site during statewide expansion.
businessImpact: A café with on-site coffee roasting or large cooking equipment in New York State may need to register or permit that air-emission source with DEC.
recommendedAction: Assess emissions of any on-site roasters/large ovens against Part 201 thresholds and register if required. / 评估店内烘焙机/大型烤箱排放是否需依第201部登记。

regulationName: Sustainable DC Omnibus Amendment Act of 2014 — Expanded Polystyrene (Foam) Food Service Ware Ban
chineseTitle: 2014年可持续华盛顿特区综合修正案 — 发泡聚苯乙烯(泡沫)餐具禁令
englishTitle: DC Foam Food Service Ware Ban
applicabilityThreshold: All DC businesses/organizations that serve food (no size threshold). / 特区内所有供应食品的企业(无规模门槛)。
keyRequirements: All District businesses and organizations that serve food are prohibited from using disposable food service ware made of expanded polystyrene (foam).
chineseSummary: 依据2014年《可持续华盛顿特区综合修正案》,自2016年1月1日起,特区内所有供应食品的企业禁止使用发泡聚苯乙烯(泡沫)一次性餐具;自2017年起还须使用可回收或可堆肥餐具;2021年起零售销售泡沫制品亦被禁。由DOEE通过检查与罚款执行,合规率超96%。
englishSummary: Under the Sustainable DC Omnibus Amendment Act of 2014, all District food-serving businesses have been banned from using disposable expanded-polystyrene (foam) food service ware since January 1, 2016, with a further requirement that disposable foodware be recyclable or compostable from 2017 and a retail foam-sales ban from 2021. The DOEE enforces it through inspections and fines, reporting over 96% business compliance.
businessImpact: A DC café must use non-foam, recyclable-or-compostable cups and containers or face DOEE inspection fines.
recommendedAction: Confirm all DC foodware is foam-free and recyclable/compostable before opening DC locations. / 进驻特区前确认餐具无泡沫且可回收/堆肥。

regulationName: Anacostia River Clean Up and Protection Act of 2009 — Disposable Bag Fee
chineseTitle: 2009年阿纳科斯蒂亚河清理与保护法 — 一次性购物袋收费
englishTitle: DC Disposable Bag Fee (Bag Law)
applicabilityThreshold: DC businesses that sell food or alcohol and provide carryout bags. / 特区内销售食品或酒类并提供购物袋的企业。
keyRequirements: District businesses that sell food or alcohol must charge a $0.05 fee on each disposable paper or plastic carryout bag, remitting most of the fee to the Anacostia River Cleanup Fund.
chineseSummary: 依据2009年《阿纳科斯蒂亚河清理与保护法》(俗称"购物袋法"),自2010年1月1日起,特区内销售食品或酒类的企业须对每个一次性纸袋或塑料袋收取5美分费用,其中4美分上缴阿纳科斯蒂亚河清理基金、1美分由商家保留。由DOEE管理执行。
englishSummary: Under the Anacostia River Clean Up and Protection Act of 2009 (the "bag law"), effective January 1, 2010, District businesses that sell food or alcohol must charge a $0.05 fee on every disposable paper or plastic carryout bag. Four cents per bag goes to the Anacostia River Cleanup Fund and one cent is retained by the retailer; the DOEE administers collection and enforcement.
businessImpact: A DC café selling food must charge and remit the 5-cent bag fee and keep records, affecting POS configuration and tax remittance.
recommendedAction: Configure DC POS to charge $0.05 per carryout bag and set up monthly fee remittance to DOEE. / 在特区POS设置每袋5美分并按月向DOEE上缴。

regulationName: Massachusetts Stretch Energy Code & Specialized Opt-In Code (225 CMR 23.00)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州延伸节能规范与专门选用规范(225 CMR 23.00)
englishTitle: MA Stretch & Specialized Energy Code (Commercial)
agency: Massachusetts Department of Energy Resources (DOER) / Board of Building Regulations and Standards
applicabilityThreshold: New construction/major renovation in MA municipalities that have adopted 225 CMR 23.00. / 已采纳225 CMR 23.00市镇的新建/大改项目。
keyRequirements: Commercial and multifamily new construction and major renovations in adopting municipalities must meet the heightened Stretch Code (and, where adopted, the net-zero-oriented Specialized Code) rather than the base energy code.
chineseSummary: 马州延伸节能规范(225 CMR 23.00,商业及多户建筑)在采纳该规范的市镇中取代基准节能规范,要求新建及大型改建达到更高能效;市镇还可加采面向近零碳的"专门选用规范"。更新版于2023年生效。是否适用取决于具体门店所在市镇是否采纳,故为逐址选用而非全州强制。
englishSummary: Massachusetts' Stretch Energy Code (225 CMR 23.00 for commercial and multifamily) raises energy-efficiency requirements above the base code for new construction and major renovations in municipalities that adopt it; cities may further adopt a net-zero-oriented Specialized Code. The updated code took effect in 2023. Applicability is municipality-by-municipality opt-in (and a prerequisite for Green Community designation), so it binds a café build-out only where the host city has adopted it.
businessImpact: A new café build-out in a Massachusetts municipality that has adopted the Stretch/Specialized Code must meet stricter HVAC, envelope, and efficiency standards, raising fit-out cost.
recommendedAction: Check each MA target municipality's code adoption before design; budget for Stretch/Specialized compliance where applicable. / 选址前核查市镇规范采纳情况并预留合规预算。

regulationName: NPDES Stormwater Construction Generic Permit (Rule 62-621.300(4), F.A.C.)
chineseTitle: NPDES暴雨水施工通用许可(F.A.C. 62-621.300(4)条)
englishTitle: Florida NPDES Construction Generic Permit (CGP)
applicabilityThreshold: Construction disturbing ≥1 acre (or part of a larger common plan) discharging stormwater. / 扰动≥1英亩(或属更大计划)且排放暴雨水的施工。
keyRequirements: Construction activity disturbing one or more acres (or less than an acre but part of a larger common plan) and discharging stormwater must obtain CGP coverage and implement a Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plan (SWPPP).
chineseSummary: 佛州环保署(FDEP)依据F.A.C. 62-621.300(4)对扰动1英亩以上土地(或属于更大开发计划一部分)且排放暴雨水的施工活动要求办理NPDES施工通用许可(CGP),须提交意向通知并实施暴雨水污染防治计划(SWPPP)。该义务多由总承包商/开发商承担,而非咖啡店运营方,仅在大型新建场地时相关。
englishSummary: Under Rule 62-621.300(4), F.A.C., the FDEP requires construction activity disturbing one or more acres (or less, if part of a larger common plan) and discharging stormwater to obtain coverage under the NPDES Construction Generic Permit and to implement a Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plan (SWPPP) with a Notice of Intent filed before work begins. The obligation typically falls on the general contractor/developer rather than the café operator and is relevant mainly for large new-build sites.
businessImpact: If a café anchors a new ground-up Florida development disturbing an acre or more, stormwater permitting and a SWPPP must be in place before construction.
recommendedAction: For Florida ground-up sites, confirm the developer/GC holds CGP coverage and a SWPPP. / 佛州新建场地须确认开发商/总包已办CGP并备SWPPP。

regulationName: NYC DEP Fats, Oils & Grease (FOG) Best Management Practices
chineseTitle: NYC DEP 油脂（FOG）最佳管理规范
englishTitle: NYC DEP Fats, Oils & Grease (FOG) BMP
applicabilityThreshold: 所有排放油脂的餐饮场所；隔油设备规模按 DEP 规定（具体门槛待核实）
keyRequirements: 安装并维护隔油器/隔油池；保留清掏记录；自 2021-09-25 起禁止食物垃圾液化器排入下水道。
chineseSummary: NYC DEP 要求餐饮场所安装并维护隔油设备以控制油脂排入下水道，并禁止使用食物垃圾液化器排放。具体规模门槛需向 DEP 核实。
englishSummary: NYC DEP requires food-service establishments to install and maintain grease interceptors to control FOG discharge, and bans food-waste-liquefier sewer discharge (since 2021-09-25). Exact size cutoffs to verify with DEP.
businessImpact: 影响门店厨房设备、隔油器维护与清掏记录。
recommendedAction: 核实各门店隔油设备规模与清掏记录是否符合 DEP 要求。

regulationName: NYC Commercial Organics Separation (Local Law 146 of 2013)
chineseTitle: NYC 商业有机垃圾分类（2013 年第 146 号地方法）
englishTitle: NYC Commercial Organics Separation (Local Law 146 of 2013)
applicabilityThreshold: 连锁餐饮：同城 2 处以上合计 8,000 平方英尺以上，或单店 7,000 平方英尺以上
keyRequirements: 源头分离有机垃圾；使用持牌运输商或现场处理；保留记录。
chineseSummary: NYC 地方法 146 要求达到规模门槛的餐饮连锁源头分离有机垃圾。瑞幸 NYC 在岗门店合计约 21,156 平方英尺，已超过 8,000 平方英尺连锁门槛。
englishSummary: NYC Local Law 146 requires covered food-service chains above the size thresholds to source-separate organics. Luckin's open NYC footprint (~21,156 sq ft combined) exceeds the 8,000 sq ft chain threshold.
businessImpact: 适用：需为 NYC 门店建立有机垃圾分类流程并签约持牌运输商。
recommendedAction: 为 NYC 门店部署有机垃圾源头分离与持牌运输商合约，并留存记录。

regulationName: NYC Commercial Waste Zones + Trade Waste Hauler Licensing (BIC)
chineseTitle: NYC 商业垃圾分区与运输商许可（BIC）
englishTitle: NYC Commercial Waste Zones and Trade Waste Hauler Licensing
applicabilityThreshold: 使用私营运输的所有商户须使用分区内 BIC 持牌运输商
keyRequirements: 使用指定商业垃圾分区内的 BIC 持牌运输商；遵守计费与服务标准。
chineseSummary: NYC 商业垃圾分区（CWZ）改革要求商户使用其所在分区内的 BIC 持牌运输商；BIC 负责运输商许可与监管。
englishSummary: NYC's Commercial Waste Zones reform requires businesses to use a BIC-licensed hauler within their assigned zone; BIC licenses and oversees private carters.
businessImpact: 影响门店垃圾清运合约选择与计费。
recommendedAction: 确认门店所在商业垃圾分区并签约 BIC 持牌运输商。

regulationName: NYC Commercial Recycling Source Separation (Local Law 87/1992)
chineseTitle: NYC 商业回收源头分类
englishTitle: NYC Commercial Recycling (Paper/Metal/Glass/Plastic)
applicabilityThreshold: 所有商业场所 All commercial establishments
keyRequirements: 将金属/玻璃/塑料与纸/纸板从垃圾中源头分离；贴标分类桶 + 张贴标识；通过私营运输商回收。
chineseSummary: NYC 要求所有商业场所将金属/玻璃/塑料与纸/纸板从垃圾中源头分离，使用贴标分类桶并张贴标识，通过私营运输商回收。这是每家门店的基础义务。
englishSummary: NYC requires all commercial establishments to source-separate metal/glass/plastic and paper/cardboard from trash, use labeled bins with posted signage, and recycle via a private carter. A baseline obligation for every store.
businessImpact: 每家门店须设置分类桶、张贴标识并合规回收。
recommendedAction: 为门店配置贴标分类桶与标识，落实回收流程。

regulationName: NYC Single-Use EPS Foam Ban (Local Law 142/2013)
chineseTitle: NYC 泡沫塑料禁令
englishTitle: NYC Expanded Polystyrene Foam Ban
applicabilityThreshold: 所有餐饮场所与商店（年收入 <$50 万可申请豁免）
keyRequirements: 不得持有、销售或提供一次性 EPS 泡沫杯、盘、托、餐盒与散装填充物。
chineseSummary: NYC 禁止餐饮场所与商店持有、销售或提供一次性发泡聚苯乙烯（EPS）泡沫杯、盘、餐盒与散装填充物。瑞幸不得使用泡沫杯/容器，须改用合规杯盖。
englishSummary: NYC bars food-service establishments and stores from possessing, selling or offering single-use expanded-polystyrene foam cups, plates, trays, clamshells and loose fill. Luckin cannot use foam cups/containers and must switch to compliant cups/lids.
businessImpact: 门店杯盖/容器不得使用泡沫塑料，须改用合规材料。
recommendedAction: 确认门店杯/盖/容器均不含 EPS 泡沫。

regulationName: NYC "Skip the Stuff" — Foodware Accessories on Request (Local Law 6/2023)
chineseTitle: NYC 餐具按需提供
englishTitle: NYC Skip-the-Stuff (Foodware Accessories on Request)
applicabilityThreshold: 提供外带/外卖的餐饮场所
keyRequirements: 除非顾客主动要求/勾选，不得随外带/外卖提供一次性餐具、餐巾、调料包。
chineseSummary: NYC「Skip the Stuff」规则要求外带/外卖除非顾客主动要求或在线勾选，否则不得附带一次性餐具、餐巾与调料包。瑞幸须更新 POS/App 默认设置为「按需提供」。
englishSummary: NYC's Skip-the-Stuff rule bars including single-use utensils, napkins and condiment packets with takeout/delivery unless the customer requests or opts in. Luckin must set POS/app defaults to request-only.
businessImpact: 门店与 App 须默认不附带餐具/餐巾，改为按需提供。
recommendedAction: 将门店与 App 外带默认改为「按需提供」餐具/餐巾。

regulationName: NYC Plastic Straw & Stirrer on Request (Local Law 64/2020)
chineseTitle: NYC 塑料吸管按需提供
englishTitle: NYC Plastic Straw-on-Request
applicabilityThreshold: 所有餐饮场所 All food-service establishments
keyRequirements: 除非顾客要求，不主动提供塑料吸管/搅拌棒；须备少量非可降解吸管以满足无障碍需求；张贴告示。
chineseSummary: NYC 要求餐饮场所除非顾客要求否则不主动提供塑料吸管与搅拌棒，但须备少量非可降解吸管以满足残障人士的无障碍需求，并张贴告示。
englishSummary: NYC requires food-service establishments not to automatically provide plastic straws/stirrers unless requested, while keeping some non-compostable straws available for accessibility (ADA) requests, with signage.
businessImpact: 门店不主动提供塑料吸管，但须保留无障碍用吸管。
recommendedAction: 门店吸管改为按需提供，保留无障碍用吸管并张贴告示。

regulationName: NYS Bag Waste Reduction Act (Plastic Bag Ban)
chineseTitle: 纽约州塑料袋减量法
englishTitle: NYS Plastic Bag Ban
applicabilityThreshold: 须收取纽约州销售税的商家
keyRequirements: 禁止一次性塑料提袋；适用地区纸袋收取 5 美分费用。
chineseSummary: 纽约州塑料袋减量法禁止提供一次性塑料提袋；在采纳纸袋费的地区纸袋收取 5 美分。瑞幸零售提袋须为纸袋或可重复使用袋。
englishSummary: NYS's Bag Waste Reduction Act bans single-use plastic carryout bags; a 5¢ paper-bag fee applies where adopted. Luckin's retail bags must be paper or reusable, not banned plastic film.
businessImpact: 门店提袋须为纸袋/可重复使用袋并处理纸袋费。
recommendedAction: 门店提袋改为纸袋/可重复使用袋，按地区处理纸袋费。

regulationName: EPA Refrigerant Management (CAA Section 608)
chineseTitle: EPA 制冷剂管理（第 608 条）
englishTitle: EPA Refrigerant Management (Section 608)
applicabilityThreshold: 服务制冷/空调设备的所有者/操作者与技师（≥50 磅充注触发泄漏修复）
keyRequirements: 不得排放制冷剂；须持证技师；大型设备泄漏修复与记录。
chineseSummary: EPA 第 608 条禁止排放制冷剂，要求由持证技师维修制冷/空调设备，对充注 ≥50 磅的大型设备适用泄漏修复与记录。咖啡店小型设备通常低于 50 磅，但不得排放的规定始终适用。
englishSummary: EPA Section 608 bans venting refrigerants, requires certified technicians for refrigeration/AC service, and applies leak-repair/recordkeeping to appliances with ≥50 lb charge. Small café units are usually below 50 lb, but the no-venting rule always applies.
businessImpact: 门店制冷/空调维修须用 EPA 持证技师；不得排放制冷剂。
recommendedAction: 使用 EPA 608 持证技师维修制冷设备并保存记录。

regulationName: EPA Antimicrobial (Sanitizer/Disinfectant) Registration — FIFRA
chineseTitle: EPA 消毒杀菌剂注册（FIFRA）
englishTitle: EPA FIFRA Sanitizer & Disinfectant Registration
applicabilityThreshold: 使用作出杀菌宣称的消毒/杀菌剂者
keyRequirements: 仅使用 EPA 注册的消毒/杀菌剂；遵循标签用法；食品接触消毒剂符合 40 CFR 180.940。
chineseSummary: FIFRA 要求仅使用 EPA 注册的消毒/杀菌剂并严格遵循标签用法与接触时间；食品接触面消毒剂须符合 40 CFR 180.940 豁免容差。门店清洁消毒须用合规产品。
englishSummary: FIFRA requires using only EPA-registered sanitizers/disinfectants per label directions and contact times; food-contact sanitizers must meet 40 CFR 180.940. Café cleaning/sanitizing must use compliant products.
businessImpact: 门店消毒/清洁须使用 EPA 注册、食品接触合规产品。
recommendedAction: 核查门店消毒剂为 EPA 注册且食品接触合规，培训按标签用法使用。

regulationName: NYC Noise Code (Equipment / HVAC)
chineseTitle: NYC 噪音法规（设备/暖通）
englishTitle: NYC Noise Code (Equipment/HVAC)
applicabilityThreshold: 所有商业场所/设备（暖通在地界 45 dB(A) 限值）
keyRequirements: 设备（暖通、压缩机）与音乐不得超过噪音限值；循环设备在地界有分贝限制。
chineseSummary: NYC 噪音法规要求暖通、压缩机等设备与播放音乐不得超过噪音限值，循环设备在地界/相邻住宅处限 45 dB(A)。屋顶/外部暖通与制冷冷凝器须达标。
englishSummary: NYC's Noise Code requires equipment (HVAC, compressors) and music not to exceed code limits, with circulation devices limited to 45 dB(A) at the property line/adjacent residence. Rooftop/exterior HVAC and refrigeration condensers must comply.
businessImpact: 门店外部暖通/制冷设备须满足地界噪音限值。
recommendedAction: 评估门店外部设备噪音是否满足 45 dB(A) 地界限值。

regulationName: NYS Returnable Container Act (Bottle Bill)
chineseTitle: 纽约州容器押金法
englishTitle: NYS Bottle Bill (Container Deposit)
applicabilityThreshold: 销售受涵盖瓶装饮料的经销商（水、汽水、啤酒、碳酸饮料；咖啡不在内）
keyRequirements: 对受涵盖容器收取 5 美分押金；接受所售饮料容器退还并对账。
chineseSummary: 纽约州容器押金法要求销售受涵盖瓶装饮料（水、汽水、碳酸饮料等）的经销商收取 5 美分押金并接受退还。即饮咖啡通常不在涵盖范围——仅当瑞幸销售受涵盖瓶装水/汽水时适用，须核实产品结构。
englishSummary: NYS's Bottle Bill requires dealers selling covered bottled beverages (water, soda, carbonated drinks) to collect a 5¢ deposit and accept returns. Ready-to-drink coffee is generally not covered — applies only if Luckin sells covered bottled water/soda; verify product mix.
businessImpact: 仅当门店销售受涵盖瓶装饮料时适用押金与退还义务。
recommendedAction: 核实是否销售受涵盖瓶装饮料；如有，落实押金与退还对账。

englishTitle: DSNY sanitation violation — POSTING OF SIGN PERMIT

regulationName: SIDEWALK OBSTRUCTION COMM MAN INDUSTRIAL MIXED USE 1ST
chineseTitle: DSNY 环卫执法 — SIDEWALK OBSTRUCTION COMM MAN INDUSTRIAL MIXED USE 1ST
englishTitle: DSNY sanitation violation — SIDEWALK OBSTRUCTION COMM MAN INDUSTRIAL MIXED USE 1ST
chineseSummary: SIDEWALK OBSTRUCTION COMM MAN INDUSTRIAL MIXED USE 1ST。罚款 $450。听证结果:DEFAULTED。
